name: 1,2-Hexanediol
explanation: A multifunctional humectant with mild antimicrobial activity — frequently used as part of preservative systems alongside phenoxyethanol or caprylyl glycol. Very well-tolerated across skin types including reactive. Not a known sensitizer.

name: 10-DECANEDIOL
explanation: A diol with mild antimicrobial activity used as a preservative booster. Well-tolerated at typical cosmetic use levels.

name: 10-HYDROXYDECANOIC ACID
explanation: A hydroxy fatty acid found in royal jelly with antimicrobial and soothing properties. Well-tolerated.

name: 2 -Hexanediol
explanation: A humectant/preservative booster (same as 2-Hexanediol, typographical variant). Well-tolerated.

name: 2-Hexanediol
explanation: A multifunctional humectant with mild antimicrobial activity — frequently used as part of preservative systems alongside phenoxyethanol or caprylyl glycol. Very well-tolerated across skin types including reactive. Not a known sensitizer.

name: 3-Butanediol
explanation: A diol humectant and solvent. Well-tolerated at typical cosmetic concentrations.

name: 3-O-Ethyl Ascorbic Acid
explanation: A stabilized Vitamin C derivative — ethylated at the 3-position to improve shelf stability while retaining brightening and antioxidant activity. Gentler and less volatile than L-ascorbic acid; well-tolerated by reactive skin.

name: 4-Terpineol
explanation: The main active component of tea tree oil with antimicrobial properties. At isolated concentrations (not as part of tea tree essential oil), generally well-tolerated.

name: 6-Naphthalate
explanation: Part of Diethylhexyl 2,6-Naphthalate — an emollient ester. Not a sensitizer.

name: Acer Saccharum Extract
explanation: Sugar maple extract — contains antioxidant polyphenols. Well-tolerated.

name: Acetyl Hexapeptide-8
explanation: Argireline — a synthetic peptide that may help relax facial muscle contractions, used in anti-wrinkle formulas. Well-tolerated; not a sensitizer.

name: Acetyl Tetrapeptide-15
explanation: A synthetic peptide that works as a firmness anchor for reactive skin — specifically targeting the extracellular matrix (the scaffolding that holds skin cells in place) to reduce the fragile, raw feeling that comes with a compromised barrier. It also has nerve-calming properties that reduce sensitivity signals at the skin surface. Very well-tolerated; the surrounding formula matters more than this ingredient.

name: Acetylated ­Glycol ­Stearate
explanation: An emollient and pearlescent agent used in creams. Not a sensitizer.

name: Acrylates Copolymer
explanation: A synthetic polymer used as a film former, thickener, and stabilizer in various cosmetic formulas. Helps give products a smooth, even texture. Not a sensitizer; purely a functional ingredient.

name: ACRYLATES/ VINYL ISODECANOATE CROSSPOLYMER
explanation: A crosslinked acrylic polymer used as a thickener and film former in sunscreens and foundations. Not a sensitizer.

name: Acrylates/Ammonium Methacrylate Copolymer
explanation: A synthetic film-forming polymer used in makeup and nail products. Inert; not a sensitizer.

name: Acrylates/C10-30 Alkyl Acrylate Crosspolymer
explanation: A carbomer-type polymer thickener. Inert; not a sensitizer.

name: Acrylates/Dimethicone Copolymer
explanation: A silicone-acrylic hybrid polymer used as a film former in makeup and hair care. Helps products adhere to skin. Inert; not a sensitizer.

name: Acrylates/Methoxy Peg-15 Methacrylate Copolymer
explanation: A PEG-modified acrylic polymer used as a film former and thickener. Not a sensitizer.

name: Acrylates/Polytrimethylsiloxymethacrylate Copolymer
explanation: A silicone-acrylic film-forming polymer used in long-wear makeup. Inert; not a sensitizer.

name: Acrylates/Stearyl Acrylate/Dimethicone Methacrylate Copolymer
explanation: A hybrid silicone-acrylic polymer used as a film former and texture modifier in long-wear and waterproof makeup. Inert on skin; not a sensitizer.

name: Acrylonitrile
explanation: A monomer found in some polymer systems in finished cosmetics (as part of a copolymer). In cured polymer form it is inert. Not a sensitizer at finished-product concentrations.

name: Acrylonitrile/Methyl Methacrylate/Vinylidene Chloride Copolymer
explanation: A hollow microsphere polymer used to create lightweight textures in makeup. Inert; not a sensitizer.

name: Actinidia Chinensis (Kiwi) Fruit Extract
explanation: Kiwi fruit extract — antioxidant-rich with vitamin C. Well-tolerated.

name: Adenosine
explanation: A nucleoside naturally found in all living cells. In skincare, it functions as an anti-aging active that may help reduce the appearance of fine lines and improve skin elasticity. Well-tolerated and non-sensitizing; no reactivity concerns.

name: Alanine
explanation: A non-essential amino acid used as a skin conditioner. Well-tolerated.

name: Alaria Esculenta Extract
explanation: Wakame seaweed extract — antioxidant and soothing. Well-tolerated.

name: Alchemilla Vulgaris Extract
explanation: Lady's mantle extract — astringent and anti-inflammatory with tannins. Well-tolerated.

name: Aleurites Moluccana Seed Oil
explanation: Kukui nut oil — lightweight, high in linoleic and alpha-linolenic acids. Used for centuries in Hawaiian skincare for dry and reactive skin. Well-tolerated.

name: algae extract
explanation: A generic term for seaweed-derived extracts with humectant, soothing, and antioxidant properties. The specific algae varies. Generally well-tolerated.

name: Algin
explanation: Sodium alginate (algae-derived) thickener and film former. Very gentle; used in peel-off masks. Well-tolerated.

name: Allantoin
explanation: A soothing, skin-conditioning ingredient derived from comfrey root (or synthesized) that calms irritated skin, supports cell turnover, and reduces hypersensitivity. It's essentially an anti-inflammatory workhorse — one of the most universally tolerated ingredients in skincare and an excellent choice for reactive skin that needs calming without challenge.

name: Aloe Barbadensis Flower Extract
explanation: Aloe vera flower extract — distinct from aloe vera leaf, lacks the aloin that causes sensitivity in some. Soothing. Well-tolerated.

name: Althaea Rosea Flower Extract
explanation: Hollyhock flower extract — contains mucilages with soothing and emollient properties. Well-tolerated.

name: Alumina
explanation: Aluminum oxide — used in cosmetics as a surface coating for pigments (improves dispersion and feel) and occasionally as a mild abrasive. Inert and non-sensitizing as used in makeup and skincare.

name: Aluminum Dimyristate
explanation: A metal soap thickener used in anhydrous formulas like stick products and oil-based sunscreens. Inert; not a sensitizer.

name: Aluminum Hydroxide
explanation: Used in cosmetics primarily as a surface coating for titanium dioxide and iron oxide pigments, improving their dispersibility and feel. Also a mild alkaline pH buffer. Inert and non-sensitizing as used in cosmetics.

name: Aluminum ­Hydroxide
explanation: Coating agent for mineral pigments. Inert in cosmetics.

name: Aluminum Starch Octenylsuccinate
explanation: A starch-based absorbent and anti-caking agent. Inert; not a sensitizer.

name: Aluminum ­Starch ­Octenylsuccinate
explanation: A starch derivative used as an absorbent and anti-caking agent in powder makeup. Inert; not a sensitizer.

name: Amber Powder
explanation: Fossilized tree resin ground into a fine powder. Used in makeup formulas as a texture modifier with soft-focus properties. Inert; not a sensitizer.

name: Aminomethyl Propanol
explanation: An alkanolamine used to neutralize acidic polymers and adjust pH. Well-tolerated at cosmetic use levels.

name: Ammonium Acryloyldimethyltaurate/VP Copolymer
explanation: A synthetic polymer used as a thickener and stabilizer in emulsions and gels — helps formulas hold their texture under varying temperature, pH, and electrolyte conditions. No active skin effects and no sensitization concerns; a purely functional texture ingredient.

name: Ammonium Polyacryloyldimethyl Taurate
explanation: A synthetic polymer thickener used in gel and emulsion formulas. Provides excellent texture stability across a wide pH range. Not a sensitizer.

name: Amodimethicone
explanation: An amino-functional silicone used as a conditioning agent. Adheres well to skin and hair. Well-tolerated.

name: Ananas Sativus (Pineapple) Fruit Extract
explanation: Pineapple fruit extract — contains bromelain enzyme with mild exfoliating properties. At extract concentrations, well-tolerated.

name: Angelica Archangelica Root Extract
explanation: Angelica root extract — antioxidant and skin-conditioning. Well-tolerated at extract concentrations.

name: Anhydroxylitol
explanation: A sugar-derived humectant (part of the aquaxyl complex with xylitol and xylitylglucoside). Helps reinforce the skin's water-holding capacity. Very well-tolerated.

name: Aqua
explanation: The INCI name for water — the base solvent for virtually all water-phase skincare formulas. Water itself has no skin concerns; the other ingredients dissolved in it are what matter.

name: Aqua/Water/Eau
explanation: Water — the universal solvent and base of most aqueous formulas. Inert.

name: Arachidyl Alcohol
explanation: A C20 fatty alcohol used as an emollient and thickener. Very gentle; compatible with sensitive-skin formulas.

name: Arachidyl Glucoside
explanation: A plant-derived glucoside emulsifier made from arachidyl alcohol and glucose. Gentle and compatible with sensitive-skin formulas.

name: Arbutin
explanation: A glycosylated hydroquinone derivative used for brightening. Flagged: arbutin hydrolyzes to hydroquinone in skin and shares its photosensitivity concern — sun exposure while using it can cause paradoxical darkening. Use with SPF; avoid in morning routines without sun protection.

name: Argania Spinosa Kernel Oil
explanation: Argan oil — a lightweight, high-oleic oil rich in vitamin E and fatty acids. Absorbs well, leaves a light skin feel, and supports barrier function. Well-tolerated; not a sensitizer. A reliable emollient for reactive skin.

name: Arginine
explanation: A semi-essential amino acid used in cosmetics as a skin-conditioning agent and pH adjuster (alkaline). Naturally found in the skin's natural moisturizing factor (NMF). Very well-tolerated; not a sensitizer.

name: Arginine HCl
explanation: Arginine hydrochloride — a salt form of the amino acid arginine used as a pH buffer and conditioner. Well-tolerated.

name: Arrowroot
explanation: Arrowroot powder — a starch used as an absorbent and texture modifier in powder formulas. Very gentle; not a sensitizer.

name: Artemisia Annua Extract
explanation: Sweet wormwood extract — antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory. Used in calming formulas. Well-tolerated.

name: Artemisia Capillaris Extract
explanation: Yin chen artemisia extract — anti-inflammatory and antioxidant botanical from Traditional Chinese Medicine. Well-tolerated.

name: Artemisia Princeps Extract
explanation: Japanese mugwort extract — a calming, anti-inflammatory botanical widely used in Korean skincare for reactive and eczema-prone skin. Very well-tolerated.

name: Artemisia Princeps Leaf Extract
explanation: Mugwort leaf extract — calming and anti-inflammatory. Well-tolerated.

name: Artemisia Princeps Leaf Powder
explanation: Mugwort leaf powder — calming, anti-inflammatory; popular in Korean skincare. Well-tolerated.

name: Artemisia Vulgaris Extract
explanation: Mugwort extract — calming and anti-inflammatory; popular in Korean skincare for reactive skin. Very well-tolerated.

name: Ascorbic Acid
explanation: Pure Vitamin C (L-ascorbic acid) — the most potent and well-studied form for brightening, collagen support, and antioxidant protection. Can cause tingling in reactive skin at higher concentrations (≥10%); start with lower percentages (5–8%) for sensitive skin. Very beneficial when tolerated.

name: Ascorbyl Palmitate
explanation: A fat-soluble ester of Vitamin C (ascorbic acid) and palmitic acid. Antioxidant that helps protect formula ingredients and skin from oxidative damage. Less irritating than pure ascorbic acid, though also less potent for brightening. Well-tolerated.

name: Asiatic Acid
explanation: A triterpene acid from centella asiatica — one of the four key CICA actives. Supports collagen synthesis, barrier repair, and has anti-inflammatory properties. Very well-tolerated; specifically studied for sensitive and compromised skin.

name: Asiatic Pennywort Extract
explanation: Centella asiatica (CICA) — a highly effective skin barrier repair and anti-inflammatory botanical. Asiaticoside, madecassoside, and other CICA compounds are specifically studied for reactive and compromised skin.

name: Asiaticoside
explanation: A triterpene saponin from centella asiatica — one of the key active compounds responsible for centella's wound-healing and skin barrier-repair properties. Stimulates collagen synthesis and calms inflammation. Very well-tolerated; specifically studied for sensitive and post-procedure skin.

name: Aspalathus Linearis Leaf Extract
explanation: Rooibos extract — rich in aspalathin and other antioxidants. Soothing. Well-tolerated.

name: Astaxanthin
explanation: A marine carotenoid antioxidant from microalgae. Among the most potent antioxidants available. Well-tolerated.

name: Avena Sativa (Oat) Kernel Extract
explanation: Oat kernel extract — contains avenanthramides and beta-glucan with soothing, anti-itch properties. Excellent for sensitive skin.

name: Avena Sativa (Oat) Kernel Flour
explanation: Finely milled oat flour — gentle physical texture agent with soothing properties. Well-tolerated.

name: Avena Sativa (Oat) Kernel Oil
explanation: Oat kernel oil — rich in linoleic acid and avenanthramides. Soothing and emollient. Excellent for sensitive skin.

name: Avena Sativa (Oat) Seed Water
explanation: Water extracted from oat seeds, rich in beta-glucan and avenanthramides — compounds with well-documented anti-inflammatory and barrier-supportive effects specifically researched for eczema-prone and reactive skin. One of the gentler ways to get oat benefits into a formula. Safe for this skin.

name: Avena Sativa Kernel Extract
explanation: Oat kernel extract — contains beta-glucan, avenanthramides, and other soothing compounds. Among the best-documented soothing actives for reactive, eczema-prone skin. Very well-tolerated.

name: Avena Sativa Meal Extract
explanation: Oat meal extract — one of the best-documented soothing actives for reactive and eczema-prone skin. Very well-tolerated.

name: Azelaic Acid
explanation: A naturally occurring acid (found in grains) that targets hyperpigmentation, acne bacteria, and rosacea simultaneously — with a significantly gentler irritation profile than stronger exfoliating acids. Some people feel a brief tingling or mild flushing when first starting it; this usually settles within a couple of weeks and is distinct from the stinging reaction this skin has to harsher actives.

name: Bakuchiol
explanation: A plant-derived retinol alternative from babchi seeds. Has similar gene-expression activity to retinol for anti-aging without photosensitivity or the irritation of retinoids. Very well-tolerated even by reactive skin — a preferred retinol alternative for sensitive skin.

name: Bambusa Vulgaris Extract
explanation: Bamboo extract — contains silica and antioxidants. Conditioning. Well-tolerated.

name: Bambusa Vulgaris Leaf /Stem Extract
explanation: Bamboo leaf/stem extract — antioxidant and conditioning. Well-tolerated.

name: Behentrimonium Methosulfate
explanation: A conditioning emulsifier — despite its name, not an irritating quaternary ammonium compound but a fatty-chain emulsifier commonly used in hair and skin creams. Reduces irritation in formulas and improves spreadability. Well-tolerated.

name: Behenyl Alcohol
explanation: A C22 fatty alcohol used as an emollient and thickener in creams and lotions. Behaves like other fatty alcohols (cetyl, stearyl) — very gentle, non-sensitizing, and skin-compatible.

name: Bentonite
explanation: A natural clay mineral used as an absorbent and thickener in masks and makeup. Inert; not a sensitizer.

name: Benzoic Acid
explanation: A preservative used in food and cosmetics — effective against yeast and bacteria. Becomes sodium benzoate in aqueous formulas. At typical cosmetic preservative concentrations it's not a practical concern. The EU requires disclosure as a fragrance allergen at higher concentrations in leave-on products, but those levels are well above what's used as a preservative.

name: Bertholletia Excelsa Seed Oil
explanation: Brazil nut oil — rich in oleic acid and selenium, with a light to medium skin feel. Emollient and nourishing. Well-tolerated; not a typical sensitizer, though tree nut allergies (rarely topical) apply to highly allergic individuals.

name: Beta-Carotene
explanation: A carotenoid antioxidant and natural orange-yellow pigment. Converted to Vitamin A in the body when ingested; topically functions as an antioxidant. Well-tolerated.

name: Beta-Glucan
explanation: A polysaccharide derived from oat, mushroom, or yeast that functions as a humectant and soothing active. Has documented wound-healing and immune-modulating properties in skin. Particularly well-suited for reactive or compromised skin; not a sensitizer.

name: Beta-Sitosterol
explanation: A plant sterol that supports barrier repair and has mild anti-inflammatory properties. Skin-compatible; well-tolerated.

name: Betaine
explanation: A naturally-derived osmolyte from sugar beets. Hydrates, soothes, and is exceptionally well-tolerated.

name: Betula Alba Juice
explanation: Birch tree sap — a hydrating botanical water with mineral content. Well-tolerated.

name: Betula Platyphylla Japonica Juice
explanation: Birch tree sap — a lightweight, mineral-rich hydrating ingredient. Well-tolerated.

name: BHA
explanation: Butylated hydroxyanisole — a synthetic antioxidant used to prevent rancidity in formula oils. Used at trace concentrations (<0.1%). Not a practical sensitization concern at these levels.

name: BHT
explanation: Butylated hydroxytoluene — a synthetic antioxidant used in cosmetics and food to prevent oxidative rancidity in formulas. Used at very low concentrations (typically <0.1%). Not a practical sensitization concern at these levels.

name: Bioactive 8 Berry Complex*
explanation: A proprietary berry extract blend. Individual berry extracts are generally well-tolerated antioxidants.

name: Biosaccharide Gum-1
explanation: A biofermented polysaccharide used as a film former and humectant with documented soothing activity. Often used in formulas targeting reactive skin. Very well-tolerated.

name: Bis-Capryloyloxypalmitamido Isopropanol
explanation: A synthetic lipid used in barrier-repair formulas. Well-tolerated.

name: Bis-Diglyceryl Polyacyladipate-2
explanation: A polyester emollient used as a lightweight, non-greasy emollient in lip products and foundations. Well-tolerated; not a sensitizer.

name: Bis-PEG-18 Methyl Ether Dimethyl Silane
explanation: A silicone-PEG emulsifier used to create water-in-silicone or silicone-in-water formulas. Well-tolerated.

name: Bis-PEG/PPG-14/14 Dimethicone
explanation: A silicone-PEG/PPG copolymer used in water-in-silicone emulsions. Well-tolerated.

name: Bisabolol
explanation: A naturally occurring alcohol from chamomile (or synthesized for stability) that's a classic anti-irritant and skin-soother. You'll often see it deliberately added to formulas alongside active ingredients to buffer their irritation potential. Very well-tolerated — a genuinely useful ingredient for reactive skin.

name: Bixa Orellana Seed Oil
explanation: Annatto seed oil — contains tocotrienols and bixin with antioxidant properties. Well-tolerated.

name: Blueberry Extract
explanation: Blueberry fruit extract — rich in anthocyanins with antioxidant properties. Well-tolerated.

name: Borago Officinalis Seed Oil
explanation: Borage seed oil — highest natural plant source of GLA (gamma-linolenic acid). GLA supports barrier repair in eczema-prone and reactive skin. Well-tolerated.

name: Borago Officinalis Seed Oil*
explanation: Borage seed oil — very high in GLA. Excellent for reactive and eczema-prone skin.

name: Boron Nitride
explanation: A synthetic mineral powder used in makeup to create a soft-focus, silk-like skin feel and reduce shine. Inert and non-sensitizing; not absorbed into skin.

name: Butyl Avocadate
explanation: An ester derived from avocado oil fatty acids, used as a lightweight emollient and skin conditioner. Significantly lighter and less occlusive than raw avocado oil. Well-tolerated and not a known sensitizer.

name: Butylene Glycol
explanation: A very common humectant and solvent that draws moisture to the skin and improves texture. Also functions as a mild antimicrobial at higher concentrations. Extremely well-tolerated — one of the most universally used cosmetic ingredients. No sensitization concerns.

name: Butylene ­Glycol
explanation: A common humectant and solvent. Very well-tolerated.

name: Butyloctyl Salicylate
explanation: An ester of salicylic acid used as an emollient — fully esterified so it has no exfoliating activity. Not a sensitizer.

name: Butyrospermum ­Parkii ­Butter
explanation: Shea butter — a rich emollient from the shea tree. Excellent barrier support for dry or reactive skin. Very well-tolerated.

name: C10-30 Alkyl Acrylate Crosspolymer
explanation: A synthetic polymer used as a thickener and emulsifier — gives formulas that lightweight, slightly watery-gel texture common in modern lotions. Performs better than carbomer in formulas with electrolytes (zinc, sodium chloride, etc.). Not a sensitizer; purely a texture ingredient.

name: C12-14 Pareth-12
explanation: A PEG-based nonionic surfactant and emulsifier. Well-tolerated.

name: C12-15 Alkyl Benzoate
explanation: A lightweight, dry-touch emollient ester. Spreads easily and leaves minimal residue. Well-tolerated; low comedogenicity.

name: C12-16 Alcohols
explanation: A blend of fatty alcohols (lauryl to cetyl). Emollient and emulsion stabilizer. Well-tolerated.

name: C12-20 Alkyl Glucoside
explanation: A sugar-based mild surfactant. Well-tolerated, commonly used in gentle cleansers.

name: C12-22 Alkyl Acrylate/Hydroxyethylacrylate Copolymer
explanation: A film-forming polymer used in long-wear makeup. Inert; not a sensitizer.

name: C13-15 Alkane
explanation: A synthetic hydrocarbon emollient — lightweight, odorless, and highly skin-compatible. Essentially a cleaner, more precisely defined alternative to mineral oil. Absorbs well, leaves a dry skin feel, and is not a sensitizer. An increasingly popular silicone alternative for lightweight formulas.

name: C14-22 Alcohols
explanation: A blend of fatty alcohols (myristyl to behenyl). Emollient and emulsion stabilizer. Well-tolerated.

name: Caffeine
explanation: Topical caffeine used for its antioxidant properties and mild vasoconstrictive effect, which may reduce puffiness in eye products. Well-tolerated; not a sensitizer. The stimulant effect does not translate to topical application.

name: Calamine
explanation: A zinc oxide and ferric oxide mixture. Classic soothing and anti-itch agent. Well-tolerated, particularly gentle for sensitive skin.

name: Calcite
explanation: Calcium carbonate in crystalline form — used as a white pigment and mild abrasive. Inert; not a sensitizer.

name: Calcium Gluconate
explanation: A calcium salt used as a skin conditioner and to support barrier function. Well-tolerated.

name: Calcium Sodium Borosilicate
explanation: Synthetic glass-based shimmer pigment used in makeup. Inert; not a sensitizer.

name: Calendula Officinalis Flower Extract
explanation: Calendula extract — well-documented anti-inflammatory and wound-healing properties. Among the most trusted botanical actives for reactive skin.

name: Camellia Japonica Flower Extract
explanation: Japanese camellia flower extract — antioxidant and conditioning. Well-tolerated.

name: Camellia Japonica Seed Oil
explanation: Camellia seed oil — rich in oleic acid with a lightweight, non-greasy feel. Traditionally used in Japanese skincare. Well-tolerated.

name: Camellia Sinensis Leaf Extract
explanation: Green tea extract, rich in antioxidants (especially EGCG) that protect against free radical damage and reduce inflammation. It has solid evidence behind it as a calming and protective ingredient with very low irritation potential — one of the safer actives to look for in formulas for reactive skin.

name: Camellia Sinensis Leaf Powder
explanation: Finely ground green tea leaf powder — provides antioxidant activity and a gentle exfoliating texture. Well-tolerated.

name: Canadian Colloidal Clay
explanation: A Canadian clay mineral used as an absorbent in masks. Inert; not a sensitizer.

name: Candida Bombicola/Glucose/Methyl Rapeseedate Ferment
explanation: A yeast ferment that produces sophorolipids — naturally-derived biosurfactants. Gentle and well-tolerated.

name: Caprylhydroxamic Acid
explanation: A chelating preservative derived from caprylic acid. Gentle and effective as part of a broad-spectrum preservation system. Well-tolerated.

name: Caprylic/Capric Glycerides
explanation: Partial glycerides of caprylic and capric acids — similar to caprylic/capric triglyceride. Lightweight emollient. Well-tolerated.

name: Caprylic/Capric Triglyceride
explanation: A lightweight emollient derived from coconut oil — essentially a purified, odorless coconut oil fraction (caprylic and capric fatty acids only) that absorbs quickly and leaves no residue. One of the most universally well-tolerated emollients in cosmetics. Non-comedogenic, shelf-stable, and appropriate for reactive skin.

name: Capryloyl Glycine
explanation: An amino acid-based surfactant with mild antimicrobial properties. Gentle and well-tolerated.

name: Caprylyl Glycol
explanation: A humectant and mild antimicrobial booster — typically paired with phenoxyethanol to enhance preservation efficacy at lower overall preservative concentrations. Draws moisture to the skin surface and gives formulas a soft, emollient feel. Very well-tolerated; not a known sensitizer.

name: Caprylyl Methicone
explanation: A silicone-hydrocarbon hybrid that reduces greasiness in oil-phase formulas and improves the skin feel of sunscreens and makeup. Non-sensitizing.

name: Caprylyl/Capryl Glucoside
explanation: A gentle plant-derived glucoside surfactant. Very mild; suitable for sensitive skin.

name: Caramel
explanation: Caramelized sugar used as a natural brown colorant in cosmetics. Inert; not a sensitizer.

name: Carbomer
explanation: A synthetic polymer (polyacrylic acid) used to thicken formulas and create gel textures. Needs to be neutralized with an alkali (sodium hydroxide) to function. Very well-tolerated — carbomer itself is not a sensitizer; reactions to carbomer-thickened formulas typically trace back to other ingredients.

name: Carica Papaya Fruit Extract
explanation: Papaya extract — contains papain enzyme with mild exfoliating properties and antioxidants. At extract concentrations the enzymatic activity is minimal. Well-tolerated.

name: Carnitine
explanation: An amino acid derivative involved in fatty acid metabolism. Used in cosmetics for conditioning. Well-tolerated.

name: Carthamus Tinctorius Seed Oil
explanation: Safflower oil — very high linoleic acid content, making it excellent for reactive, barrier-compromised skin. Light texture. Well-tolerated.

name: Carum Petroselinum (Parsley) Extract
explanation: Parsley extract (common name variant). Antioxidant and anti-inflammatory. Well-tolerated.

name: Carum Petroselinum Extract
explanation: Parsley extract — contains apigenin and vitamin C with brightening and antioxidant properties. Well-tolerated.

name: Cassia Tora Seed Extract
explanation: Sickle senna seed extract — soothing and conditioning. Well-tolerated.

name: Castanea Crenata Shell Extract
explanation: Chestnut shell extract — antioxidant and astringent. Well-tolerated.

name: caulerpa lentillifera extract
explanation: Sea grape algae extract — contains polysaccharides and antioxidants. Well-tolerated.

name: Cellulose
explanation: Plant-derived cellulose used as a thickener, absorbent, and texture agent. Inert and non-sensitizing.

name: Cellulose Gum
explanation: Carboxymethylcellulose — a plant-derived thickener widely used in food and cosmetics. Creates smooth gel textures. Very well-tolerated.

name: Centaurea Cyanus Flower Extract
explanation: Cornflower extract — soothing and antioxidant. Traditionally used in eye-area products. Well-tolerated.

name: Centella Asiatica
explanation: Cica — a plant extract that's become central to K-beauty for its wound-healing and barrier-building properties. The active compounds (asiaticoside, madecassoside) calm inflammation and stimulate collagen production. This is the kind of ingredient designed for exactly this skin type: it rebuilds the barrier rather than challenging it.

name: Centella Asiatica Extract
explanation: A concentrated extract from Centella Asiatica standardized for its active triterpenoids. It reduces inflammation, accelerates skin barrier repair, and has clinical backing specifically for sensitive and reactive skin. One of the more reliably helpful actives for this skin type.

name: Centella Asiatica Leaf Extract
explanation: Centella asiatica leaf extract — a variant of the full centella extract with the same soothing, wound-healing actives (madecassoside, asiaticoside). Well-tolerated.

name: Centella Asiatica Leaf Water
explanation: Distilled centella leaf water — a hydrating botanical water with mild centella actives. Well-tolerated.

name: Cera Microcristallina/Microcrystalline Wax
explanation: Microcrystalline wax — a refined petroleum-derived wax used in lip products and makeup to adjust texture and consistency. Less occlusive than petrolatum; not a sensitizer.

name: Ceramide
explanation: Ceramides are lipids that make up roughly half of the skin's barrier structure — they're what holds skin cells together and keeps moisture from escaping. Supplementing them topically helps rebuild a compromised barrier. Extremely well-tolerated, and one of the most fundamentally useful ingredients for reactive skin that needs structural repair.

name: Ceramide 3
explanation: Ceramide 3 (also called ceramide NP) is one of the most abundant ceramides in healthy skin, critical for barrier integrity and moisture retention. Topical application helps replenish what's lost through age, harsh products, or environmental stress. Excellent tolerability, and genuinely restorative for reactive and sensitized skin.

name: Ceramide AP
explanation: One of the nine ceramide types found in human skin, playing a role in maintaining barrier structure and preventing water loss. Topical application helps repair barrier damage from within. Well-tolerated and beneficial for reactive skin that needs structural support.

name: Ceramide EOP
explanation: A long-chain ceramide found in the outermost layer of skin, important for the water-impermeability of the barrier structure. Depleted by age, harsh cleansers, and environmental exposure — replenishing it topically helps restore barrier function. Excellent tolerability.

name: Ceramide NP
explanation: The most common ceramide type in skin, essential for forming the lamellar structure that makes the barrier water-tight. The key ingredient in CeraVe and similar barrier-repair formulas. Universally well-tolerated and actively beneficial for reactive or compromised skin.

name: Ceramides
explanation: The lipid class that forms the backbone of the skin's moisture barrier. When ceramides are depleted — by age, harsh products, or environmental damage — skin becomes dry, reactive, and easily irritated. Replenishing them topically is one of the most evidence-backed approaches to barrier repair for reactive skin.

name: Ceresin
explanation: A refined natural mineral wax (ozocerite derivative) used to structure lip products and makeup sticks. Less occlusive than petrolatum; not a sensitizer.

name: cereus grandiflorus flower extract
explanation: Night-blooming cereus flower extract — antioxidant and conditioning. Well-tolerated.

name: Ceteareth-20
explanation: A PEG-based emulsifier made from cetearyl alcohol. Used to create stable oil-in-water emulsions. Well-tolerated at typical cosmetic use levels; not a significant sensitizer.

name: Ceteareth-60 Myristyl Glycol
explanation: A PEG-based emulsifier used in stable oil-in-water emulsions. Well-tolerated.

name: Cetearyl Alcohol
explanation: A fatty alcohol — completely different from ethanol or isopropyl alcohol despite the shared name. It's a waxy, solid material from cetyl and stearyl fatty acids that thickens emulsions and adds a creamy texture. Widely used in sensitive-skin formulas; no stinging, no barrier disruption.

name: Cetearyl Dimethicone/Vinyl Dimethicone Crosspolymer
explanation: A crosslinked silicone elastomer with a cushiony texture. Inert; not a sensitizer.

name: Cetearyl Glucoside
explanation: A plant-derived emulsifier made from cetearyl alcohol and glucose. Gentle and biodegradable; often used in natural and sensitive-skin formulas. No sensitization concerns.

name: Cetearyl Isononanoate
explanation: A lightweight emollient ester. Dry, non-greasy skin feel. Not a sensitizer.

name: Cetearyl Olivate
explanation: An emulsifier derived from cetearyl alcohol and olive oil. Pairs with sorbitan olivate to form the Olivem 1000 emulsifier system, known for its skin-like lipid profile and compatibility with sensitive skin.

name: Cetyl Alcohol
explanation: A fatty alcohol — completely different from ethanol or isopropyl alcohol. A waxy, solid material derived from coconut or palm that thickens emulsions and adds a creamy, moisturizing texture. Widely used in sensitive-skin formulas; not a sensitizer or barrier disruptor.

name: Cetyl Dimethicone
explanation: A long-chain silicone used as an emollient and conditioning agent. Well-tolerated.

name: Cetyl Ethylhexanoate
explanation: An emollient ester that gives formulas a lightweight, silky skin feel. Spreads easily and absorbs without greasiness. Well-tolerated; not a sensitizer.

name: Cetyl PEG/PPG-10/1 Dimethicone
explanation: A silicone-based emulsifier used in water-in-silicone formulas — the backbone of many lightweight BB creams and sunscreens with a non-greasy finish. Well-tolerated; not a sensitizer.

name: Cetyl ­PEG/PPG-­10/1 ­Dimethicone
explanation: A silicone-based emulsifier used in water-in-silicone formulas. Well-tolerated.

name: Chamaecyparis Obtusa Leaf Extract
explanation: Hinoki cypress leaf extract — antimicrobial and soothing. Well-tolerated at extract concentrations.

name: Chamomilla Recutita (Matricaria) Flower Extract
explanation: Chamomile flower extract — soothing and anti-inflammatory. Identical to Chamomilla recutita matricaria flower extract. Well-tolerated.

name: Chamomilla Recutita Flower Extract
explanation: German chamomile flower extract — contains bisabolol and chamazulene, which have well-documented anti-inflammatory and skin-soothing properties. One of the most trusted soothing actives for reactive skin. Very well-tolerated; rarely causes sensitization despite being a botanical.

name: Chamomilla Recutita Matricaria Flower Extract
explanation: German chamomile extract — soothing, anti-inflammatory. One of the most trusted botanical actives for reactive skin.

name: Charcoal Powder
explanation: Activated charcoal used as an absorbent in cleansers and masks. Inert; not a sensitizer.

name: Chenopodium Quinoa Seed Extract
explanation: Quinoa seed extract — contains proteins and amino acids with conditioning properties. Well-tolerated.

name: chlorella minutissima extract
explanation: Microalgae extract — rich in proteins, amino acids, and antioxidants. Well-tolerated.

name: Chlorella Vulgaris Extract
explanation: Green microalgae extract — rich in chlorophyll, amino acids, and antioxidants. Well-tolerated.

name: Chlorphenesin
explanation: A mild preservative with antibacterial and antifungal activity. Often paired with phenoxyethanol or glycols to broaden spectrum coverage at lower concentrations. Well-tolerated; not a significant sensitizer at typical cosmetic use levels.

name: Cholesterol
explanation: Cholesterol is one of the three key lipids that form the skin barrier (alongside ceramides and fatty acids) — literally part of the skin's own structural matrix. Topical application helps restore the lamellar structure of a compromised barrier. Extremely well-tolerated and actively beneficial for reactive or damaged skin.

name: Chondrus Crispus
explanation: Irish moss / carrageenan — seaweed thickener with soothing properties. Well-tolerated.

name: Chondrus Crispus Extract
explanation: Irish moss (carrageenan) extract — a seaweed-derived thickener and hydrocolloid with skin-soothing properties. Very well-tolerated.

name: Ci 15850 / Red 7 Lake
explanation: D&C Red 7 Lake — a synthetic lake dye used in lip and face products. Approved for cosmetic use. Inert.

name: Ci 16035
explanation: Red 40 — a synthetic dye approved for cosmetic use. Inert.

name: Ci 16035/Red 40
explanation: Red 40 — approved cosmetic colorant. Inert.

name: Ci 19140
explanation: Yellow 5 (Tartrazine) — a synthetic yellow dye approved for cosmetic use. Inert at typical levels.

name: Ci 19140/Yellow 5
explanation: Yellow 5 — approved cosmetic colorant. Inert.

name: Ci 42090
explanation: Blue 1 (Brilliant Blue FCF) — a synthetic dye used to tint cosmetic products. Approved by both FDA and EU for cosmetic use. Inert at typical use levels; not a sensitizer.

name: Ci 77007/Ultramarines
explanation: Ultramarine blue/violet/pink — synthetic mineral pigments used in makeup. Inert and non-sensitizing.

name: Ci 77491
explanation: Iron Oxide Red — a mineral pigment used in makeup to create red, pink, and warm-toned shades. Inert, non-sensitizing, and non-comedogenic.

name: Ci 77492
explanation: Iron Oxide Yellow — a mineral pigment used in makeup and tinted skincare to create warm and golden tones. Inert, non-sensitizing, and non-comedogenic.

name: Ci 77499
explanation: Iron Oxide Black — a mineral pigment used in makeup to create dark brown, grey, and black shades. Inert, non-sensitizing, and non-comedogenic.

name: Ci 77499 / Iron Oxides
explanation: Black iron oxide — a mineral pigment used in makeup. Inert and non-sensitizing.

name: Ci 77499/Iron Oxides
explanation: Iron Oxide Black (Ci 77499) — a mineral pigment used in makeup. Inert, non-sensitizing, and non-comedogenic.

name: Ci 77891
explanation: Titanium Dioxide (Ci 77891) — the INCI colorant code for TiO2. Mineral UV filter and white pigment. Inert and non-sensitizing.

name: Ci 77891 / Titanium Dioxide
explanation: Titanium dioxide — mineral UV filter and pigment. Inert and non-sensitizing.

name: Ci 77891/Titanium Dioxide
explanation: Titanium dioxide (Ci 77891) — the preferred mineral UV filter for reactive skin. Physically reflects UV light without any chemical reaction in skin. Also listed as "Ci 77891" in some labeling conventions.

name: Cinnamomum Cassia Bark Extract
explanation: Cinnamon bark extract — antioxidant and antimicrobial. At extract concentrations, cinnamaldehyde levels are much lower than in the essential oil. Generally well-tolerated at cosmetic concentrations.

name: Citric Acid
explanation: A weak acid used primarily to adjust pH in formulas — lowering it to the skin's natural range of roughly 4.5–5.5. At the low concentrations used for pH adjustment, it has no meaningful exfoliating activity. Only acts as an AHA exfoliant at deliberately high concentrations in specialty treatments.

name: Citric ­Acid
explanation: pH adjuster. No exfoliating activity at pH-adjustment concentrations.

name: Citrullus Lanatus Fruit Extract
explanation: Watermelon fruit extract — contains lycopene and citrulline with antioxidant and hydrating properties. Well-tolerated.

name: Citrus Aurantifolia (Lime) Fruit Extract
explanation: Lime fruit extract (not oil) — antioxidant with vitamin C. At extract concentrations, phototoxic compounds are not meaningfully present. Well-tolerated.

name: Citrus Aurantium Dulcis Fruit Extract
explanation: Sweet orange fruit extract (not oil) — contains hesperidin and vitamin C. No meaningful phototoxicity risk at extract concentrations. Well-tolerated.

name: Citrus Limon Fruit Extract
explanation: Lemon fruit extract — distinct from lemon peel oil. The extract contains vitamin C and flavonoids but lacks the phototoxic furanocoumarins concentrated in the peel oil. At typical use levels, no meaningful photosensitivity risk. Well-tolerated.

name: Citrus Reticulata Peel Extract
explanation: Mandarin peel extract — an extract (not essential oil), so much of the volatile phototoxic material is removed in processing. Contains hesperidin and flavonoids. At typical extract concentrations, not a significant photosensitivity concern. Well-tolerated.

name: Citrus Tangerina (Tangerine) Extract
explanation: Tangerine fruit extract — antioxidant. At extract concentrations, not phototoxic. Well-tolerated.

name: Cl 42090 Blue 1
explanation: Blue 1 colorant — approved for cosmetic use. Inert at typical levels.

name: Clitoria Ternatea Flower Extract
explanation: Butterfly pea flower extract — rich in anthocyanins with antioxidant properties. Well-tolerated.

name: Cocamidopropyl Betaine
explanation: An amphoteric surfactant derived from coconut oil. Much gentler than SLS/SLES — used in baby shampoos and sensitive-skin cleansers. Reduces the irritation potential of stronger surfactants when combined. Well-tolerated by most reactive skin.

name: Cocamidopropyl Hydroxysultaine
explanation: A mild amphoteric surfactant similar to cocamidopropyl betaine. Conditioning; used in gentle cleansers.

name: Coccinia Indica Fruit Extract
explanation: Ivy gourd fruit extract — a plant used in Ayurvedic tradition with antioxidant and soothing properties. Used in calming skincare formulas. Well-tolerated.

name: Coco Glucoside
explanation: A very gentle plant-derived surfactant from coconut and glucose. Among the mildest cleansing agents available.

name: Coco-Betaine
explanation: An amphoteric surfactant similar to cocamidopropyl betaine, derived from coconut oil. Gentle and conditioning; reduces the irritation potential of stronger surfactants. Well-tolerated by reactive skin.

name: Coco-Glucoside
explanation: A very gentle plant-derived surfactant made from coconut and glucose. Compatible with sensitive, eczema-prone, and baby skin. Among the mildest cleansing agents available.

name: Coconut Acid
explanation: A blend of fatty acids from coconut oil (mainly lauric and myristic acid). Used as an emulsifier base. Well-tolerated.

name: Cocos Nucifera Fruit Extract
explanation: Coconut fruit extract — conditioning and soothing. Well-tolerated.

name: Coffea Arabica (Coffee) Seed Extract
explanation: Coffee seed extract — rich in chlorogenic acids and caffeine with antioxidant properties. Well-tolerated.

name: Coffea Arabica Seed Extract
explanation: Coffee seed (bean) extract — contains chlorogenic acid and caffeine with antioxidant and mild vasoconstrictive effects. Used in eye and body-firming products. Well-tolerated.

name: Coix Lacryma-Jobi Ma-Yuen Seed Extract
explanation: Job's tears seed extract — soothing and brightening, used in Asian skincare. Well-tolerated.

name: Colloidal Oatmeal
explanation: Finely ground oats suspended in liquid — an FDA-recognized skin protectant with clinical evidence for reducing itch and inflammation in eczema and reactive skin. The beta-glucan and avenanthramide content forms a protective film while actively calming the inflammatory response. One of the most validated soothing ingredients for reactive skin.

name: Colloidal Oatmeal (2%) Water
explanation: Colloidal oatmeal suspension — the FDA-recognized active for skin protection and itch relief. Excellent for sensitive and eczema-prone skin.

name: Copper Gluconate
explanation: A copper salt used in trace amounts. Copper is a cofactor for skin enzymes involved in collagen synthesis and antioxidant defense. Well-tolerated.

name: Copper ­Gluconate
explanation: A water-soluble copper salt that delivers copper ions to skin — the same mineral that makes copper peptides work. Think of copper as the fire extinguisher for reactive skin: decades of research confirm it mutes inflammation, accelerates healing in irritated clusters, and actively rebuilds barrier integrity in skin that's felt thinned or raw. A precursor form to the more potent copper peptides.

name: Copper Tripeptide-1
explanation: Copper bound to a three-amino-acid peptide (GHK-Cu) — the fire extinguisher for reactive skin. This is one of the most researched skin-repair ingredients available: it mutes inflammation, accelerates healing of irritated red patches, and rebuilds the "thinned" skin barrier that makes reactive skin feel so raw and exposed. Originally developed for wound care, which tells you something about how seriously it takes barrier repair.

name: Copper Tripeptide-1
explanation: Copper bound to a three-amino-acid peptide (GHK-Cu) — the fire extinguisher for reactive skin. This is one of the most researched skin-repair ingredients available: it mutes inflammation, accelerates healing of irritated red patches, and rebuilds the "thinned" skin barrier that makes reactive skin feel so raw and exposed. Originally developed for wound care, which tells you something about how seriously it takes barrier repair.

name: Coptis Japonica Root Extract
explanation: Japanese goldthread root extract — contains berberine with antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory properties. Used in calming formulas. Well-tolerated.

name: Corallina Officinalis Extract
explanation: Red algae (Corallina) extract — a marine-derived ingredient with antioxidant, skin-soothing, and mild film-forming properties. Used primarily for its conditioning and smoothing effects. Low sensitization potential; well-tolerated.

name: Cordyceps Sinensis Extract
explanation: Cordyceps mushroom extract — antioxidant and energizing. Well-tolerated.

name: Corylus Avellana Seed Oil
explanation: Hazelnut oil — very high in oleic acid with a light, non-greasy feel. Well-tolerated; non-comedogenic.

name: Cucumis Sativus Fruit Extract
explanation: Cucumber fruit extract — soothing and hydrating, often used in eye and calming products. Contains flavonoids and amino acids. Very well-tolerated; not a sensitizer.

name: Curcuma Longa Root Extract
explanation: Turmeric root extract — anti-inflammatory and antioxidant activity from curcuminoids, with genuine research behind it for calming inflammatory skin conditions. Used at low concentrations in cosmetics; well-tolerated. Can temporarily tint skin yellow at high concentrations, but amounts in cleansing formulas are too small for that.

name: Cyclohexane
explanation: A cyclic hydrocarbon used as a processing solvent. Typically fully evaporated before the product is finished; not expected to be present in the final formula at meaningful levels.

name: Cyclohexasiloxane
explanation: A cyclic volatile silicone (D6) similar to cyclopentasiloxane. Used as a lightweight carrier that evaporates after application. Subject to EU concentration limits in rinse-off products due to environmental concerns; safe on skin.

name: Cyclomethicone
explanation: A blend of cyclic volatile silicones (D4/D5/D6). Evaporates after application. Well-tolerated; D4 and D5 have EU environmental restrictions in rinse-off products.

name: Cyclopentasiloxane
explanation: A cyclic volatile silicone (D5) used as a lightweight carrier and sensory enhancer that evaporates off skin after application. Gives formulas a silky, non-greasy slip. Well-tolerated; subject to EU concentration limits in rinse-off products due to environmental accumulation concerns, but safe on skin.

name: Cymbidium Grandiflorum Flower Extract
explanation: Orchid flower extract — used for its conditioning and antioxidant properties. Well-tolerated.

name: Cynara Scolymus Leaf Extract
explanation: Artichoke leaf extract — contains cynarin and chlorogenic acid with antioxidant and brightening properties. Well-tolerated.

name: Daucus Carota Sativa Root Extract
explanation: Carrot root extract — contains beta-carotene and other carotenoids with antioxidant properties. Well-tolerated.

name: Decyl Glucoside
explanation: A gentle, plant-derived surfactant made from glucose and decanol. One of the mildest cleansing agents available — compatible with reactive, eczema-prone, and baby skin. No significant sensitization concerns.

name: Decyl Isostearate
explanation: A lightweight emollient ester with a non-greasy skin feel. Well-tolerated; low comedogenicity.

name: Decylene Glycol
explanation: A mild preservative booster and skin-conditioning agent. Gentle alternative to traditional preservatives. Well-tolerated.

name: Dextrin
explanation: A starch-derived thickener and adhesive used in makeup and sunscreen formulas. Inert and non-sensitizing.

name: Dextrin Palmitate
explanation: A starch ester used to thicken oil-phase formulas and improve texture. Inert; not a sensitizer.

name: Dibutyl Adipate
explanation: A lightweight emollient ester with a dry, non-greasy finish. Used in lip products and foundations. Well-tolerated.

name: Dicaprylyl Carbonate
explanation: A lightweight emollient carbonate ester that gives formulas a silky, dry-touch skin feel similar to silicones — without the environmental accumulation concerns. Absorbs quickly with no greasy residue. Very well-tolerated.

name: Dicaprylyl Ether
explanation: A lightweight emollient that gives a very dry, silky skin feel. Used as a silicone alternative. Not a sensitizer.

name: Diethylhexyl 2
explanation: An emollient ester with excellent skin feel — used in makeup and sunscreens. Not a sensitizer.

name: Diglycerin
explanation: A glycerin dimer — humectant with excellent skin compatibility. Well-tolerated.

name: Diisopropyl Sebacate
explanation: A lightweight emollient ester used as a skin conditioner and spreading agent. Gives products a thin, dry-touch feel. Well-tolerated; not a sensitizer.

name: Diisostearyl Malate
explanation: An ester derived from malic acid and isostearic alcohol. Used as a lightweight emollient in lip products and makeup, providing gloss and skin comfort. Well-tolerated.

name: Dimer Dilinoleyl Dimer Dilinoleate
explanation: A complex polyester emollient used in lip products and makeup. Non-sticky; very well-tolerated.

name: Dimethicone
explanation: A silicone polymer used as an emollient, skin protectant, and sensory modifier. Creates a smooth, slip-enhanced skin feel and forms a light, breathable barrier that reduces transepidermal water loss without truly occluding the skin. Very well-tolerated — silicones are among the least reactive cosmetic ingredients.

name: Dimethicone/PEG-10/15 Crosspolymer
explanation: A crosslinked dimethicone-PEG hybrid used as a thickener and emulsifier in silicone-based formulas. Gives a smooth, cushiony texture. Not a sensitizer.

name: Dimethicone/Silsesquioxane Copolymer
explanation: A hybrid silicone polymer used for its smooth skin feel and film-forming properties. Well-tolerated.

name: Dimethicone/Vinyl Dimethicone Crosspolymer
explanation: A crosslinked silicone elastomer that gives formulas a smooth, cushiony, pore-filling texture. Used in primers and foundations. Non-sensitizing; purely a sensory ingredient.

name: Dimethiconol
explanation: A hydroxyl-terminated silicone used as an emollient and conditioning agent. Well-tolerated.

name: dimethiconol fluoroalcohol dilinoleic acid.
explanation: A fluorosilicone compound used to improve water and sebum resistance in makeup formulas. Not a sensitizer.

name: Dimethyl Sulfone
explanation: MSM (methylsulfonylmethane) — an organic sulfur compound with anti-inflammatory properties. Well-tolerated.

name: Dioctyl Sodium Sulfosuccinate
explanation: A mild sulfosuccinate surfactant. Much gentler than SLS. Well-tolerated in cleansing formulas.

name: Dioscorea Japonica Root Extract
explanation: Japanese yam root extract — contains diosgenin and polysaccharides with moisturizing and soothing properties. Used in hydrating formulas. Well-tolerated.

name: Dipentaerythrityl Tetrahydroxystearate/Tetraisostearate
explanation: A complex ester emollient used in anhydrous makeup for a rich, smooth feel. Not a sensitizer.

name: Diphenylsiloxy Phenyl Trimethicone
explanation: A phenyl silicone with higher refractive index than standard dimethicone, used in makeup for a natural-finish appearance and enhanced gloss. Well-tolerated; not a sensitizer.

name: Dipotassium Glycyrrhizate
explanation: The potassium salt of glycyrrhizic acid from licorice root. A reliable soothing active that calms redness and reduces inflammatory responses in reactive skin. Well-tolerated and specifically favored in formulas for sensitive and eczema-prone skin.

name: Dipotassium Phosphate
explanation: A phosphate salt used as a pH buffer in cosmetic formulas. Purely a stability and pH-management ingredient; not a sensitizer.

name: Dipropylene Glycol
explanation: A humectant and solvent used to disperse fragrances and other actives in formulas. Gentler than propylene glycol; well-tolerated across skin types. Also commonly used as a fragrance carrier, though the fragrance dissolved in it may be the relevant factor to watch.

name: Disodium Cocoamphodiacetate
explanation: An amphoteric surfactant derived from coconut oil. Gentle and conditioning; reduces irritation of other surfactants in the formula. Used in sensitive-skin cleansers.

name: Disodium Cocoyl Glutamate
explanation: An amino acid-based (glutamic acid) mild surfactant derived from coconut. Very gentle; suitable for sensitive-skin cleansers.

name: Disodium EDTA
explanation: A chelating agent that binds trace metal ions in water, preventing them from degrading other ingredients and affecting formula stability. Used at very low concentrations; no meaningful skin-active properties and no sensitization concerns.

name: Disodium ­EDTA
explanation: Chelating agent. Stabilizes formulas. Not a sensitizer.

name: Disodium Laureth Sulfosuccinate
explanation: A mild sulfosuccinate-based surfactant — significantly gentler than SLS/SLES despite the similar sound. One of the preferred cleansing agents for sensitive-skin formulas because of its low irritation profile.

name: Disodium Stearoyl Glutamate
explanation: A gentle emulsifier derived from glutamic acid and stearic acid. Amino acid-based emulsifiers are among the mildest available; this one is often used in formulas specifically targeting sensitive skin. No sensitization concerns.

name: Disteardimonium Hectorite
explanation: A clay-based thickener and suspending agent used in anhydrous formulas (foundations, lip products, oil-based sunscreens). Keeps pigments and minerals suspended evenly. Inert and non-sensitizing.

name: Eclipta Prostrata Extract
explanation: Bhringraj extract — anti-inflammatory with traditional use in Ayurvedic hair and skin care. Well-tolerated.

name: Eclipta Prostrata Leaf Extract
explanation: Bhringraj leaf extract — anti-inflammatory. Well-tolerated.

name: EDTA
explanation: Ethylenediaminetetraacetic acid — the parent chelating agent of disodium/tetrasodium EDTA forms. Binds metal ions to stabilize formulas. Not a sensitizer.

name: Elaeis Guineensis (Palm) Kernel Oil
explanation: Palm kernel oil — high in lauric acid with emollient properties. Well-tolerated.

name: Elaeis Guineensis (Palm) Oil
explanation: Palm oil — rich in palmitic and oleic acid. Emollient; well-tolerated.

name: Epilobium Angustifolium Flower/Leaf/Stem Extract
explanation: Willowherb extract — potent anti-inflammatory; reduces redness and supports reactive skin. Very well-tolerated.

name: Epilobium Fleischeri Flower/Leaf/Stem Extract
explanation: Alpine willowherb extract — contains oenothein B and other polyphenols with anti-inflammatory and sebum-regulating properties. Used in calming formulas for reactive skin. Well-tolerated.

name: Ergosterol
explanation: A plant/fungal sterol (provitamin D2). Supports barrier function similarly to cholesterol. Well-tolerated.

name: Erythritol
explanation: A sugar alcohol used as a humectant. Well-tolerated.

name: Ethoxydiglycol
explanation: A glycol ether used as a solvent and penetration enhancer. Helps active ingredients absorb into skin. Well-tolerated at typical cosmetic concentrations.

name: Ethyl Hexanediol
explanation: A diol used as a humectant and mild insect repellent. Well-tolerated at cosmetic concentrations.

name: Ethylhexyl Hydroxystearate
explanation: A hydroxylated emollient ester with a smooth skin feel. Well-tolerated.

name: Ethylhexyl Isononanoate
explanation: A lightweight emollient ester with a dry, non-greasy skin feel. Used in sunscreens and foundations. Well-tolerated.

name: Ethylhexyl Palmitate
explanation: A synthetic ester of 2-ethylhexanol and palmitic acid used as a skin conditioner and emollient. Gives formulas a dry, velvety skin feel without greasiness. Has a moderate comedogenicity rating — some acne-prone people find it contributes to clogged pores, but most skin types tolerate it fine. Not a sensitizer.

name: Ethylhexyl Stearate
explanation: A lightweight emollient ester with a dry, non-greasy feel. Well-tolerated.

name: Ethylhexylglycerin
explanation: A glycerin-derived preservative booster and skin conditioner. Well-tolerated.

name: Eucalyptus Globulus Leaf Extract
explanation: Eucalyptus leaf extract — distinct from eucalyptus essential oil. At extract concentrations, the 1,8-cineole content is much lower. Mild antimicrobial. Generally well-tolerated.

name: Euphorbia Cerifera Wax
explanation: Candelilla wax — a plant-derived wax from candelilla shrubs. Common vegan alternative to beeswax in lip products. Not a sensitizer.

name: Evening Primrose Extract
explanation: Evening primrose plant extract — contains GLA and other beneficial fatty acids in extract form. Well-tolerated.

name: Ferric Ammonium Ferrocyanide
explanation: Prussian blue (Ci 77510) — a blue pigment used in cosmetics. Inert; not a sensitizer.

name: Ferulic Acid
explanation: A hydroxycinnamic acid antioxidant found in plant cell walls. Significantly boosts the efficacy of vitamins C and E. Well-tolerated; not a sensitizer.

name: Ficus Carica Fruit Extract
explanation: Fig fruit extract — contains flavonoids and enzymes with conditioning properties. Well-tolerated.

name: Fragaria Chiloensis Fruit Extract
explanation: Wild strawberry extract — antioxidant. Well-tolerated.

name: Fructooligosaccharides
explanation: Prebiotic sugars that feed beneficial bacteria on the skin microbiome. Well-tolerated and non-sensitizing.

name: Fructose
explanation: A natural sugar used as a humectant and osmolyte. Well-tolerated.

name: Fruit Stem Cells (Malus Domestica Fruit Cell Culture Extract
explanation: Apple stem cell extract — contains plant epigenetic factors. Well-tolerated.

name: Ganoderma Lucidum Extract
explanation: Reishi mushroom extract — contains beta-glucans and triterpenes with antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties. Well-tolerated.

name: Garcinia Mangostana Peel Extract
explanation: Mangosteen peel extract — contains xanthones with potent antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties. Well-tolerated.

name: Gardenia Florida Fruit Extract
explanation: Gardenia fruit extract — contains crocin and other antioxidants. Well-tolerated.

name: Gaultheria Procumbens (Wintergreen) Leaf Extract
explanation: Wintergreen leaf extract — contains methyl salicylate with analgesic and antimicrobial properties. At diluted extract concentrations, generally well-tolerated.

name: Ginkgo Biloba Leaf Extract
explanation: Ginkgo leaf extract — contains flavonoids and terpenoids with antioxidant and mild anti-inflammatory activity. Well-tolerated in skincare; not a common sensitizer at cosmetic concentrations.

name: Ginko Biloba Leaf Extract
explanation: Ginkgo biloba leaf extract (alternate spelling) — antioxidant and circulation-boosting. Well-tolerated.

name: Gluconolactone
explanation: A polyhydroxy acid (PHA) — a gentler class of chemical exfoliant with a larger molecular size than AHAs, meaning it doesn't penetrate as deeply and causes much less irritation. Also functions as a humectant and antioxidant. Mildly increases sun sensitivity, so morning use warrants SPF. Very well-tolerated even by reactive skin; a useful step up from plain humectants for people who can't tolerate glycolic or lactic acid.

name: Glucose
explanation: A simple sugar used as a humectant and skin-conditioning agent. Also serves as a nutrient source in fermentation-based products. Completely inert and non-sensitizing.

name: Glutathione
explanation: A cellular antioxidant tripeptide used in brightening and antioxidant formulas. Very well-tolerated.

name: Glycereth-26
explanation: A PEG-modified glycerin used as a humectant and emollient. Draws moisture to skin and improves product texture. Well-tolerated; not a sensitizer.

name: Glycerin
explanation: A humectant that draws moisture from the environment (and from deeper skin layers) to the surface — one of the most studied and universally well-tolerated skincare ingredients. It works across all skin types including reactive, helps barrier function at higher concentrations, and improves the delivery of other ingredients. A safe and genuinely useful staple.

name: Glycerine
explanation: Alternate spelling of glycerin — same ingredient, same safety profile. Well-tolerated.

name: Glyceryl Acrylate/Acrylic Acid Copolymer
explanation: A hydrophilic polymer that acts as a film former and humectant. Well-tolerated.

name: Glyceryl Behenate
explanation: A glycerin ester of behenic acid used as a thickener and emollient in solid formulas. Well-tolerated.

name: Glyceryl Caprylate
explanation: A gentle emulsifier and mild antimicrobial preservative booster derived from glycerin and caprylic acid. Very well-tolerated; often used in natural and sensitive-skin formulas as a gentler alternative to parabens.

name: Glyceryl Cocoate
explanation: A gentle emulsifier made from glycerin and coconut fatty acids. Well-tolerated.

name: Glyceryl Glucoside
explanation: A glycerin-glucose conjugate that enhances aquaporin activity, improving the skin's water transport. Very well-tolerated; specifically studied for dehydrated and reactive skin.

name: Glyceryl Laurate
explanation: A gentle emulsifier and mild antimicrobial made from glycerin and lauric acid. Well-tolerated at typical cosmetic use levels.

name: Glyceryl Stearate
explanation: An emulsifier made from glycerin and stearic acid that helps oil and water phases blend and stay stable in emulsions. Very gentle; frequently used in sensitive-skin formulas specifically because of its low irritation profile. No known sensitization concerns.

name: Glycine
explanation: The simplest amino acid — used as a skin conditioner and buffer. Well-tolerated.

name: Glycine Max Seed Extract
explanation: Soybean seed extract — contains isoflavones and lecithin with antioxidant and conditioning properties. Well-tolerated.

name: Glycine Soja (Soybean) Oil
explanation: Soybean oil — a linoleic acid-rich emollient. Well-tolerated; non-comedogenic.

name: Glycine Soja Oil
explanation: Soybean oil — a lightweight, high-linoleic emollient that supports the skin barrier. Well-tolerated; soy sensitivity via topical application is rare and distinct from food allergy.

name: Glycine Soja Seed Extract
explanation: Soybean seed extract — contains isoflavones with antioxidant and conditioning properties. Well-tolerated.

name: Glycol Distearate
explanation: A stearic acid ester used as a pearlescent agent and emollient in cleansers and lotions. Well-tolerated.

name: Glycyrrhiza Glabra Root Extract
explanation: Licorice root extract — contains glabridin and licochalcone A with potent anti-inflammatory and brightening (tyrosinase-inhibiting) properties. Among the best-tolerated botanical actives for reactive and redness-prone skin.

name: Glycyrrhiza Uralensis Root Extract
explanation: Chinese licorice root extract — similar to Glycyrrhiza glabra; contains glycyrrhizin and liquiritin with potent anti-inflammatory and brightening (tyrosinase-inhibiting) properties. Well-tolerated.

name: Green 5
explanation: A synthetic colorant approved for cosmetic use. Inert at typical use levels.

name: Green Tea
explanation: Green tea — rich in EGCG catechins with potent antioxidant and anti-inflammatory activity. Very well-tolerated.

name: Green Tea Extract
explanation: Green tea extract — rich in EGCG and other catechins with potent antioxidant and anti-inflammatory activity. Very well-tolerated; among the most studied and trusted botanical actives.

name: Guar Hydroxypropyltrimonium Chloride
explanation: A modified guar gum used as a conditioning and film-forming agent. Provides a smooth, soft skin feel. Well-tolerated; not a significant sensitizer at typical cosmetic use levels.

name: Haematococcus Pluvialis Oil
explanation: Astaxanthin-rich microalgae oil — one of the most potent natural antioxidants. Well-tolerated.

name: Hamamelis Virginiana Extract
explanation: Witch hazel extract — the extract (not the alcohol-based distillate) retains tannins and other polyphenols with astringent and antioxidant properties without the alcohol-based irritation risk. Well-tolerated at typical concentrations.

name: Hamamelis Virginiana Leaf Extract
explanation: Witch hazel leaf extract — gentler than the distillate (which contains alcohol). Retains tannin-based astringent and antioxidant properties. Well-tolerated.

name: Hdi/Trimethylol Hexyllactone Crosspolymer
explanation: A polyurethane-based film-forming polymer used in long-wear makeup to improve adhesion and flexibility. Inert on skin; not a sensitizer.

name: Helianthus Annus Seed Oil*
explanation: Sunflower seed oil — lightweight, high-linoleic, excellent for reactive skin. Well-tolerated.

name: Helianthus Annuus (Sunflower) Seed Oil
explanation: Sunflower seed oil — high in linoleic acid, lightweight and well-suited to reactive skin. Well-tolerated.

name: Helianthus Annuus Seed Oil
explanation: Sunflower seed oil — lightweight, rich in linoleic acid (omega-6), and one of the better-researched oils for supporting the skin barrier. High-linoleic oils are recommended for barrier-compromised and reactive skin because linoleic acid deficiency is associated with eczema. Absorbs quickly without heaviness.

name: Helianthus Annuus Seed Wax
explanation: Sunflower seed wax — a plant-derived wax used as a thickener and texture modifier. Very mild; not a sensitizer.

name: Hexapeptide-11
explanation: A yeast-derived peptide working as a firmness anchor — part of the peptide family that strengthens the extracellular matrix to make skin feel denser and less fragile. It improves texture and resilience over time. Very well-tolerated, consistent with the peptide class generally.

name: Hexapeptide-9
explanation: A synthetic peptide that signals skin to produce more collagen by mimicking a collagen fragment — building the firmness anchor from within. It strengthens the extracellular matrix over time, which is what makes skin feel less raw and reactive even when nothing is actively irritating it. Gentle and appropriate for reactive skin.

name: Hexylene Glycol
explanation: A glycol used as a humectant, solvent, and surfactant coupling agent. Well-tolerated at typical cosmetic concentrations; occasionally reported as a mild irritant at very high concentrations in occupational settings, but not at typical cosmetic use levels.

name: Hibiscus Esculentus Fruit Extract
explanation: Okra fruit extract — contains mucilages that provide a soothing, film-forming effect. Well-tolerated.

name: Hibiscus Sabdariffa Flower Extract
explanation: Roselle hibiscus extract — rich in anthocyanins with antioxidant properties. Well-tolerated.

name: Hippophae Rhamnoides Fruit Extract
explanation: Sea buckthorn fruit extract — extremely rich in vitamins C and E, carotenoids. Antioxidant. Well-tolerated.

name: Hippophae Rhamnoides Oil
explanation: Sea buckthorn berry oil — very rich in carotenoids (orange color), tocopherols, and palmitoleic acid. Potent for skin repair. Well-tolerated.

name: Honey Extract
explanation: Honey-derived conditioning and humectant ingredient. Contains enzymes and amino acids. Well-tolerated.

name: Honey Extract*
explanation: Honey-derived conditioning and humectant. Well-tolerated.

name: Hordeum Distichon Extract
explanation: Barley extract — contains beta-glucan and other conditioning compounds. Well-tolerated.

name: Houttuynia Cordata Extract
explanation: Heartleaf (Houttuynia cordata) extract — a cooling, anti-inflammatory plant extract central to Korean skincare for acne-prone and sensitive skin. Has documented antimicrobial activity and helps calm redness and irritation. The key ingredient in Anua's cleansing oil line. Well-tolerated with no common allergen concerns.

name: Hyacinthus Orientalis Extract
explanation: Hyacinth flower extract — conditioning and antioxidant. Well-tolerated at cosmetic concentrations.

name: Hydrocolloid
explanation: A gel-forming material used in medical wound dressings and in acne pimple patches. It creates a moist healing environment that draws fluid out of blemishes and protects the wound from bacteria and contact. The same technology used in post-surgical wound care — excellent tolerability and a non-chemical approach to spot treatment that works well for reactive skin.

name: Hydrogen Dimethicone
explanation: A reactive silicone that crosslinks to form films or elastomers in formulas. Non-sensitizing.

name: Hydrogenated Castor Oil
explanation: Castor oil hardened by hydrogenation — used as a wax-like thickener and emollient in lip products and creams. Well-tolerated; not a sensitizer.

name: Hydrogenated Castor Oil Isostearate
explanation: A castor oil derivative used as an emollient in lip products. Not a sensitizer.

name: Hydrogenated Coconut Oil
explanation: Coconut oil that has been partially or fully hydrogenated to create a solid wax-like emollient. More stable than raw coconut oil and less comedogenic than some coconut fractions. Well-tolerated.

name: Hydrogenated Farnesene
explanation: A hydrogenated terpene-derived emollient with a light, dry skin feel. Used as a silicone alternative. Not a sensitizer.

name: Hydrogenated Jojoba Oil
explanation: Jojoba oil solidified by hydrogenation — used as a wax-like emollient and thickener. Shares the excellent skin compatibility of jojoba.

name: Hydrogenated Lecithin
explanation: A stabilized form of lecithin (phospholipid emulsifier) — identical in function to regular lecithin but more resistant to oxidation. Phospholipids are natural components of cell membranes and are highly skin-compatible. Also acts as a skin-conditioning agent that supports barrier integrity. Very well-tolerated.

name: Hydrogenated Poly
explanation: Hydrogenated polyisobutene — a lightweight synthetic emollient that gives formulas a silky, non-greasy skin feel. Often used as a silicone alternative. Not a sensitizer.

name: Hydrogenated Polydecene
explanation: A synthetic hydrocarbon emollient with a smooth, spreadable skin feel. Not a sensitizer.

name: Hydrogenated Polyisobutene
explanation: A lightweight synthetic emollient that gives a silky, non-greasy feel. Used as a silicone and petrolatum alternative. Not a sensitizer.

name: Hydrogenated ­Polyisobutene
explanation: A lightweight synthetic emollient. Silky, non-greasy. Not a sensitizer.

name: Hydrogenated Vegetable Oil
explanation: A solidified plant oil used as an emollient. Generally well-tolerated.

name: Hydrolyzed Collagen
explanation: Collagen protein broken into small peptide fragments that condition skin and act as humectants. Cannot rebuild collagen (too large to penetrate), but effective as a surface moisturizer. Well-tolerated.

name: Hydrolyzed Corn Starch
explanation: Partially broken-down corn starch that functions as a humectant and skin conditioner. Well-tolerated.

name: Hydrolyzed Glycosaminoglycans
explanation: Hydrolyzed glycosaminoglycans — a family of sugar-protein molecules (includes chondroitin sulfate, dermatan sulfate) broken into smaller fragments that condition skin and signal repair processes. Functions as a skin-conditioning humectant. Unlike hyaluronic acid (also a glycosaminoglycan), this blend has lower moisture-trapping intensity and is well-tolerated.

name: Hydrolyzed Jojoba Esters
explanation: Jojoba wax broken into smaller fragments — a conditioning, skin-compatible emollient. Very well-tolerated.

name: Hydrolyzed Myrtus Communis Leaf Extract
explanation: Myrtle leaf extract — antioxidant and antimicrobial. Well-tolerated.

name: Hydrolyzed Vegetable Protein
explanation: Hydrolyzed plant protein used as a humectant and film former. Generally well-tolerated; those with specific legume allergies should check the source protein.

name: Hydroxyacetophenone
explanation: A phenolic antioxidant and preservative booster that enhances the efficacy of other preservatives. Also has mild anti-inflammatory properties. Well-tolerated; not a known sensitizer.

name: hydroxyapatite
explanation: A naturally occurring mineral form of calcium phosphate — the same mineral that makes up bone and tooth enamel. Used in skincare for its soothing and remineralizing properties. Well-tolerated.

name: Hydroxyethyl Acrylate/Sodium Acryloyldimethyl Taurate Copolymer
explanation: A synthetic polymer used as a thickener and emulsion stabilizer — gives formulas a smooth, lightweight gel texture. Commonly found in modern serums and lightweight moisturizers. Not a sensitizer.

name: Hydroxyethyl Urea
explanation: A modified urea that functions as a humectant without the keratolytic activity of urea at higher concentrations. Very well-tolerated.

name: Hydroxyethylcellulose
explanation: A cellulose-derived thickener and film former used in gels and serums. Gives formulas a clean, non-tacky gel texture. Very well-tolerated; not a sensitizer.

name: Hydroxyethylpiperazine Ethane Sulfonic Acid
explanation: HEPES — a biological pH buffer used to maintain stable pH in formulas. Inert; not a sensitizer.

name: Hydroxypropyl Cyclodextrin
explanation: A cyclic sugar molecule used to encapsulate fragrance and active ingredients, improving stability and controlled release. Inert; not a sensitizer.

name: Hydroxypropyl Methylcellulose
explanation: A cellulose ether used as a thickener and film former. Inert; very well-tolerated.

name: Hydroxypropyl Starch Phosphate
explanation: A modified starch used as a thickener and absorbent. Inert; not a sensitizer.

name: Hydroxypropyltrimonium Hyaluronate
explanation: A cationically modified hyaluronic acid with improved adhesion to skin and hair surfaces. Provides the same humectant benefits as standard HA with better substantivity. Well-tolerated.

name: Hydroxypropyltrimonium Hydrolyzed Wheat Protein
explanation: A cationic wheat protein derivative used for hair and skin conditioning. Well-tolerated.

name: Hydroxypropyltrimonium Hydrolyzed Wheat Starch
explanation: A cationic wheat starch derivative used for conditioning. Well-tolerated.

name: Hypochlorous Acid
explanation: A weak acid produced naturally by white blood cells as part of the immune response to infection — it's literally part of how the body fights bacteria. In skincare, it's used as a gentle antimicrobial spray that's particularly popular in the eczema and reactive-skin community. Very low irritation potential; it actively helps calm inflammatory skin conditions rather than challenge them.

name: Illicium Verum (Anise) Fruit Extract
explanation: Star anise fruit extract (common name variant of Illicium Verum Fruit Extract). Well-tolerated.

name: Illicium Verum Fruit Extract
explanation: Star anise extract — contains anethole and antioxidants. Well-tolerated at extract concentrations.

name: Illite
explanation: A clay mineral used as an absorbent in masks and powder makeup. Inert; not a sensitizer.

name: Inulin Lauryl Carbamate
explanation: A prebiotic-derived film former that supports skin microbiome balance. Well-tolerated.

name: Iron Oxide Black
explanation: Iron Oxide Black (Ci 77499) — mineral pigment used in darker makeup shades. Inert and non-sensitizing.

name: Iron Oxide Red
explanation: Iron Oxide Red (Ci 77491) — mineral pigment for red-warm tones in makeup. Inert and non-sensitizing.

name: Iron Oxide Yellow
explanation: Iron Oxide Yellow (Ci 77492) — a mineral pigment used in makeup. Inert and non-sensitizing.

name: Iron Oxide/Ci 77491
explanation: Iron Oxide Red (Ci 77491) — mineral pigment. Inert and non-sensitizing.

name: Iron Oxides
explanation: Mineral pigments (Ci 77491 red, Ci 77492 yellow, Ci 77499 black) used to create every shade of makeup from nude to deep brown. Inert, non-sensitizing, and non-comedogenic. The preferred pigment base for reactive skin.

name: Isoamyl Laurate
explanation: A lightweight emollient ester derived from isoamyl alcohol and lauric acid. Dry skin feel; not a sensitizer.

name: Isobutane
explanation: A hydrocarbon propellant used in aerosol products. Evaporates completely on dispensing; no residue on skin. Not a sensitizer.

name: Isoceteth-10
explanation: A PEG-based emulsifier and solubilizer. Well-tolerated.

name: Isododecane
explanation: A lightweight branched hydrocarbon solvent that gives makeup formulas a fast-drying, non-greasy finish. Evaporates off skin, leaving a smooth, comfortable wear. Not a sensitizer.

name: Isohexadecane
explanation: A lightweight branched hydrocarbon emollient that gives formulas a fast-spreading, non-greasy skin feel. Used in foundations and sunscreens. Not a sensitizer.

name: Isononyl Isononanoate
explanation: A lightweight branched emollient ester. Very dry, silky skin feel. Not a sensitizer.

name: Isopropyl Isostearate
explanation: An emollient ester with a light skin feel. Moderate comedogenicity rating; not a sensitizer.

name: Isopropyl Lauroyl Sarcosinate
explanation: An amino acid-derived (sarcosine) emollient ester with a dry, non-greasy feel. Well-tolerated.

name: Isopropyl Myristate
explanation: An emollient ester with a dry, penetrating skin feel. Has a moderate comedogenicity rating and may contribute to clogged pores in acne-prone skin, but is not a sensitizer and is well-tolerated by most skin types including reactive.

name: Isopropyl Palmitate
explanation: An emollient ester of isopropyl alcohol and palmitic acid — gives formulas a dry, non-greasy skin feel. Rated moderately comedogenic on the classic 0–5 scale (typically 4), meaning it can contribute to clogged pores in acne-prone skin. Not a sensitizer; the concern is primarily pore-clogging rather than reactive skin symptoms.

name: Isopropyl Titanium Triisostearate
explanation: A dispersing agent used in makeup to help spread titanium dioxide and pigments evenly. Present at trace concentrations; not a sensitizer.

name: Isotridecyl Isononanoate
explanation: A lightweight emollient ester with a dry, non-greasy skin feel. Well-tolerated.

name: Jasminum Officinale Extract/Jasmine Flower Extract
explanation: Jasmine flower extract — at extract concentrations, much less sensitizing than jasmine essential oil. Used for soothing and antioxidant properties. Well-tolerated for most.

name: Jojoba Esters
explanation: Hydrogenated jojoba wax — used as a wax alternative and emollient. Inherits jojoba's excellent skin compatibility. Well-tolerated.

name: Kaolin
