explanation: A naturally occurring white clay used as an absorbent, thickener, and opacifier. Gentle and very well-tolerated — frequently used in sensitive-skin masks. Not a sensitizer.

name: Lactobacillus Ferment
explanation: A ferment from Lactobacillus bacteria that contains antimicrobial peptides, acids, and other beneficial metabolites. Helps support skin microbiome balance and provides mild preservation activity. Very well-tolerated.

name: Lactobacillus Ferment Lysate
explanation: Lysed Lactobacillus bacteria — probiotic-derived components that support the skin microbiome. Well-tolerated.

name: Lactobacillus*
explanation: Lactobacillus bacteria or its ferment — probiotic-derived benefits for skin microbiome support. Well-tolerated.

name: Lactobacillus/Eriodictyon Californicum Ferment Extract
explanation: A ferment combining Lactobacillus with yerba santa plant. The fermentation process produces beneficial acids and peptides. Well-tolerated.

name: Lactobacillus/Pumpkin Ferment Extract
explanation: A ferment of Lactobacillus with pumpkin — conditioning and microbiome-supportive. Well-tolerated.

name: Lactobacillus/Soybean Ferment Extract
explanation: A ferment of Lactobacillus and soybean — produces beneficial metabolites including amino acids and vitamins. Well-tolerated.

name: Lake Ci 77007 / Ultramarines
explanation: Ultramarine blue/violet — a mineral pigment derived from lapis lazuli. Inert and non-sensitizing.

name: Laminaria Digitata Extract
explanation: Kelp extract — contains fucoidan and minerals with hydrating and soothing properties. Well-tolerated.

name: Laminaria Japonica Extract
explanation: Kelp extract — contains fucoidan and iodine with soothing and conditioning properties. Well-tolerated.

name: LAMINARIA SACCHARINA EXTRACT
explanation: Sugar kelp extract — contains alginates and fucoidan with soothing and humectant properties. Well-tolerated.

name: Laureth-4
explanation: A PEG-based emulsifier. Well-tolerated at typical cosmetic concentrations.

name: Laureth-7
explanation: A PEG-derived emulsifier and surfactant used to stabilize emulsions and disperse oils. Well-tolerated at typical cosmetic use levels.

name: Lauric Acid
explanation: A C12 saturated fatty acid naturally found in coconut and palm kernel oil. Used as an emulsifier and antimicrobial agent. Skin-identical; well-tolerated, though its medium-chain length means a moderate comedogenicity rating.

name: Lauroyl Lysine
explanation: An amino acid derivative (lysine + lauric acid) used as a slip agent and surface modifier in powder makeup. Creates a silky, adherent feel. Well-tolerated; not a sensitizer.

name: Lauryl Alcohol
explanation: A C12 fatty alcohol used as an emollient and consistency agent. Well-tolerated.

name: Lauryl Glucoside
explanation: A gentle plant-derived glucoside surfactant from coconut and glucose. Compatible with sensitive skin.

name: Lauryl Hydroxysultaine
explanation: A mild amphoteric surfactant similar to betaines. Conditioning and low-irritation; used in sensitive-skin cleansers.

name: Lauryl PEG-10 Trissilylethyl Dimethicone
explanation: A silicone-based emulsifier. Well-tolerated.

name: Lauryl PEG-8 Dimethicone
explanation: A silicone-PEG emulsifier used in water-in-silicone formulas. Well-tolerated.

name: Lauryl PEG-9 Polydimethylsiloxyethyl Dimethicone
explanation: A silicone-PEG emulsifier used in lightweight water-in-silicone formulas. Well-tolerated; not a sensitizer.

name: Lauryl Polyglyceryl-3 Polydimethylsiloxyethyl Dimethicone
explanation: A silicone-polyglycerol hybrid emulsifier. Well-tolerated.

name: Lavandula Angustifolia Flower Extract
explanation: Lavender flower extract — distinct from lavender essential oil. The extract has much lower linalool content and sensitization risk. Well-tolerated.

name: Lecithin
explanation: A phospholipid derived from soy or egg yolk. Excellent skin compatibility — structurally similar to skin's own lipids.

name: Lens Esculenta Fruit Extract
explanation: Lentil extract — contains proteins and minerals. Conditioning. Well-tolerated.

name: Lentinus Edodes Extract
explanation: Shiitake mushroom extract — antioxidant and conditioning. Well-tolerated.

name: Leptospermum Scoparium Leaf Extract
explanation: Manuka leaf extract — antimicrobial and soothing properties from the manuka plant (distinct from manuka honey). Well-tolerated.

name: Leptospermum Scoparium Leaf Extract*
explanation: Manuka leaf extract — antimicrobial and soothing. Well-tolerated.

name: Leucine
explanation: An essential branched-chain amino acid used in cosmetics for conditioning. Well-tolerated.

name: Lilium Candidum Bulb Extract
explanation: White lily bulb extract — used for its brightening and skin-smoothing properties. Well-tolerated at cosmetic concentrations.

name: Limnanthes Alba Seed Oil
explanation: Meadowfoam seed oil — an exceptionally stable emollient oil with an unusual fatty acid profile (>95% C20-22 fatty acids). Absorbs well, extends shelf life of other oils in a formula, and is very well-tolerated by reactive skin.

name: Linum Usitatissimum Seed Oil*
explanation: Flaxseed oil — very high in alpha-linolenic acid (ALA, omega-3). Good for dry, reactive skin. Well-tolerated.

name: Lonicera Japonica Flower Extract
explanation: Honeysuckle flower extract — contains chlorogenic acid and luteolin with antimicrobial and antioxidant properties. Often used as a natural preservative booster. Well-tolerated.

name: Lycium Barbarum Fruit Extract
explanation: Goji berry extract — rich in polysaccharides and zeaxanthin with antioxidant and hydrating properties. Well-tolerated.

name: Lysine
explanation: An essential amino acid used in cosmetics for skin conditioning. Well-tolerated.

name: Macadamia Ternifolia Seed Oil
explanation: Macadamia seed oil — rich in palmitoleic acid, a fatty acid naturally found in young skin that decreases with age. More emollient and richer than lighter oils like sunflower. Well-tolerated; not a sensitizer. Sits on the heavier end of lightweight oils — best in rinse-off or overnight products if the formula feels too rich.

name: Madecassic Acid
explanation: A triterpene acid from centella asiatica — one of the four key CICA actives alongside asiaticoside, asiatic acid, and madecassoside. Supports barrier repair and has anti-inflammatory properties. Very well-tolerated; specifically studied for sensitive and compromised skin.

name: Madecassoside
explanation: One of the key active compounds extracted from Centella Asiatica (cica) — a triterpenoid with strong anti-inflammatory and wound-healing properties and evidence for stimulating collagen synthesis. It builds the skin barrier rather than stressing it, which makes it one of the more appropriate actives for this skin type.

name: Magnesium
explanation: In skincare, magnesium typically arrives as a salt form used for its anti-inflammatory and calming properties. The evidence for topical magnesium effects is less robust than for oral supplementation, but it's very well-tolerated topically and unlikely to trigger any reaction, even on reactive skin.

name: Magnesium Gluconate
explanation: A magnesium salt used for skin conditioning and barrier support. Well-tolerated.

name: Magnesium Sulfate
explanation: Epsom salt — used in bath soaks and masks for mild anti-inflammatory properties and temporary skin texture improvement. Very well-tolerated and commonly used in eczema and reactive-skin care. An accessible, low-risk way to incorporate a calming mineral soak into a routine.

name: Malpighia Glabra Fruit Extract
explanation: Acerola cherry extract — one of the richest natural sources of Vitamin C. Antioxidant and brightening. Well-tolerated.

name: Malt Extract
explanation: Barley malt extract — contains sugars, amino acids, and B vitamins. Conditioning humectant. Well-tolerated.

name: Maltodextrin
explanation: A starch-derived thickener and encapsulation aid. Inert; not a sensitizer.

name: Malva Sylvestris Extract
explanation: Common mallow extract — contains mucilages with soothing and emollient properties. Well-tolerated.

name: Malva Sylvestris Flower Extract
explanation: Common mallow flower extract — soothing mucilages. Well-tolerated.

name: Manganese ­Gluconate
explanation: A manganese mineral salt used in trace amounts. Cofactor for antioxidant enzymes. Well-tolerated.

name: Mangifera Indica (Mango) Fruit Extract
explanation: Mango fruit extract — antioxidant-rich. Well-tolerated.

name: Mannose
explanation: A simple sugar (monosaccharide) used as a humectant and skin-conditioning agent. Also has prebiotic properties that may support skin microbiome balance. Very well-tolerated.

name: Matricaria Flower Extract
explanation: Chamomile flower extract — the same soothing botanical as Chamomilla recutita, listed by its genus name. Well-tolerated.

name: Matrixyl
explanation: A trade name for a specific peptide complex (usually palmitoyl pentapeptide-4) — the original firmness anchor peptide ingredient. It stimulates collagen and elastin production by telling skin it needs to repair itself, strengthening the extracellular matrix that makes skin feel dense and resilient rather than fragile. Well-tolerated and one of the better-researched cosmetic peptides.

name: Melaleuca Alternifolia Leaf Extract
explanation: Tea tree leaf extract — distinct from tea tree essential oil. The extract retains mild antibacterial properties without the concentrated terpene load that makes the oil a sensitization risk. Well-tolerated.

name: Melatonin
explanation: A potent antioxidant hormone. Topically, it has antioxidant and anti-inflammatory effects. Well-tolerated.

name: Melia Azadirachta Bark Extract
explanation: Neem bark extract — antibacterial, anti-inflammatory, and astringent. Used in small amounts as part of the neem plant complex. Well-tolerated at typical cosmetic concentrations.

name: Melia Azadirachta Flower Extract
explanation: Neem flower extract — the mildest of the neem plant derivatives, with antimicrobial and skin-conditioning properties and a lighter odor than neem seed oil. Anti-bacterial and calming. Well-tolerated at typical cosmetic concentrations.

name: Melia Azadirachta Leaf Extract
explanation: Neem leaf extract — antibacterial, anti-inflammatory, and antifungal. More potent than the flower extract, with research supporting use in acne-prone and reactive skin. Well-tolerated at typical cosmetic concentrations; people with known tree nut sensitivities occasionally react to neem-family ingredients.

name: Mentha Arvensis Extract
explanation: Field mint leaf extract — distinct from the essential oil. The extract has much lower menthol content and mild antimicrobial/cooling properties without the sensitization risk of the oil. Well-tolerated at extract concentrations.

name: Mentha Piperita Extract/Peppermint Extract
explanation: Peppermint leaf extract — at extract concentrations, much lower menthol load than peppermint oil. Mild cooling and antimicrobial properties. Tolerated by most; those with menthol sensitivity should patch-test.

name: Methicone
explanation: A lower-molecular-weight silicone used as a lightweight skin conditioner. Leaves a smooth, non-greasy feel. Well-tolerated; not a sensitizer.

name: Methyl Gluceth-20
explanation: A PEG-modified methyl glucose derivative used as a humectant and emollient. Draws moisture to skin and improves formula texture. Well-tolerated.

name: Methyl Trimethicone
explanation: A volatile silicone (MT) used as a lightweight carrier. Evaporates quickly after application, leaving other formula components on skin. Well-tolerated.

name: Methyl/Methacrylate
explanation: A copolymer component used in film-forming systems. Inert in finished products.

name: Mica
explanation: A naturally occurring mineral used to add shimmer and luminosity to makeup products. Inert, non-sensitizing, and non-comedogenic. The shimmer mineral of choice for reactive skin.

name: Microcrystalline Cellulose
explanation: A cellulose-derived absorbent and thickener used in pressed powders and cream formulas. Inert and non-sensitizing.

name: Montmorillonite
explanation: A clay mineral and the main component of bentonite. Absorbent; inert and non-sensitizing.

name: Morinda Citrifolia Seed Oil
explanation: Noni seed oil — a lightweight emollient rich in fatty acids. Well-tolerated.

name: Moringa Oleifera Seed Oil
explanation: Moringa seed oil — a lightweight emollient pressed from moringa tree seeds, historically valued in perfumery for its stability and skin compatibility. Rich in oleic and behenic acid; absorbs cleanly. Well-tolerated and not a sensitizer.

name: Myristic Acid
explanation: A saturated fatty acid found naturally in skin lipids and in plant oils such as coconut. Used as an emulsifier and skin conditioner. Skin-identical and very well-tolerated; no sensitization concerns.

name: Myristyl Alcohol
explanation: A C14 fatty alcohol used as an emollient and thickener. Gentle; compatible with sensitive-skin formulas.

name: N-Decanoyl Serinol
explanation: A fatty acid-amino alcohol conjugate used as an emollient and skin-conditioning agent. Well-tolerated.

name: Nelumbo Nucifera Extract
explanation: Lotus extract — antioxidant and soothing. Well-tolerated.

name: Nelumbo Nucifera Flower Extract
explanation: Lotus flower extract — soothing and antioxidant. Well-tolerated.

name: Nelumbo Nucifera Root Extract
explanation: Lotus root extract — antioxidant and soothing. Well-tolerated.

name: Neopentyl Glycol Diethylhexanoate
explanation: A lightweight emollient ester that gives formulas a dry, velvety skin feel. Very low comedogenicity; well-tolerated.

name: Neopentyl Glycol Diheptanoate
explanation: A lightweight emollient ester with a dry skin feel. Well-tolerated; low comedogenicity.

name: Niacinamide
explanation: Vitamin B3 — one of the most versatile and well-researched skincare ingredients available. It reduces pore visibility, fades hyperpigmentation, strengthens the skin barrier, and has meaningful anti-inflammatory effects. Generally very well tolerated; a small number of people experience temporary flushing at concentrations above 10%, but at 5% it's almost universally problem-free.

name: Nonapeptide-1
explanation: A synthetic peptide that may modulate melanin production for mild brightening. Well-tolerated.

name: Nordihydroguaiaretic Acid
explanation: A potent plant-derived antioxidant (from the creosote bush) that inhibits lipid oxidation. Used at very low concentrations to stabilize formulas. Not a sensitizer at these levels.

name: Nylon-12
explanation: A synthetic polyamide powder used in makeup to create a soft-focus, blurring effect and to give pressed powders their texture. Inert and non-sensitizing; not absorbed into skin.

name: Nymphaea Alba Flower Extract
explanation: White water lily flower extract — contains flavonoids and other polyphenols with antioxidant and soothing properties. Well-tolerated.

name: O-Cymen-5-Ol
explanation: A synthetic phenol derivative with antimicrobial activity. Used as a gentle preservative, often in combination with other preservation systems. Well-tolerated at typical cosmetic use levels.

name: Ocimum Sanctum Leaf Extract
explanation: Holy basil (tulsi) leaf extract — an anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial plant extract from Ayurvedic tradition. Its active compounds calm reactive skin and help reduce microbial load. Used in small amounts in cleansing oils and serums; well-tolerated with no common sensitization concerns.

name: Octyldodecanol
explanation: A branched-chain fatty alcohol used as a lightweight emollient and co-solvent. Gives products a silky, non-greasy feel. Well-tolerated; low comedogenicity.

name: Octyldodecyl Stearoyl Stearate
explanation: A complex ester emollient used in lip products and makeup for a smooth, non-greasy feel. Not a sensitizer.

name: Oenothera Biennis Flower Extract
explanation: Evening primrose flower extract — soothing. Well-tolerated.

name: Oenothera Biennis Oil
explanation: Evening primrose oil — very high in GLA (gamma-linolenic acid), an omega-6 fatty acid with documented benefits for eczema and dry, reactive skin. GLA helps restore the barrier lipid balance. Well-tolerated.

name: Oenothera Biennis Oil*
explanation: Evening primrose oil — high in GLA. Well-tolerated; beneficial for reactive skin.

name: Olea Europaea Fruit Oil
explanation: Olive oil — a rich, high-oleic emollient with antioxidant properties (squalene, polyphenols). Safe and well-tolerated for most. Note that its high oleic acid content (~73%) makes it a heavier oil that sits on the skin surface rather than absorbing deeply — some research suggests high-oleic oils may be less well-suited for very compromised or eczema-prone barriers. Lighter linoleic-acid oils (sunflower, grape seed) may work better during reactive flares.

name: Olea Europaea Husk Oil
explanation: Olive husk oil — extracted from olive pomace; emollient properties similar to virgin olive oil. Well-tolerated.

name: Olea Europea Fruit Oil
explanation: Olive oil (typographic variant) — a rich emollient with antioxidant properties. Well-tolerated.

name: Oleanolic Acid
explanation: A pentacyclic triterpene found in olive, lilac, and many other plants. Has anti-inflammatory and mild sebum-regulating properties. Used in pore-minimizing and brightening formulas. Well-tolerated.

name: Oleic Acid
explanation: A monounsaturated fatty acid naturally found in skin and many plant oils. Used as an emollient and penetration enhancer. Well-tolerated.

name: Olive Squalene
explanation: Squalene from olive — identical to squalane but in its unsaturated form. Skin-identical (naturally found in sebum) and very well-tolerated. More prone to oxidation than squalane.

name: Orbignya Oleifera Seed Oil
explanation: Babassu oil — lightweight emollient from an Amazonian palm. Rich in lauric acid; non-greasy. Well-tolerated.

name: Origanum Vulgare Extract
explanation: Oregano extract — antimicrobial and antioxidant. Well-tolerated at extract concentrations.

name: Origanum Vulgare Leaf Extract
explanation: Oregano leaf extract — antimicrobial and antioxidant. Well-tolerated at extract concentrations.

name: Oryza Sativa Bran Oil
explanation: Rice bran oil — contains ferulic acid and gamma-oryzanol with antioxidant properties. Lightweight emollient. Well-tolerated.

name: Oryza Sativa Cera
explanation: Rice bran wax — a plant-derived wax used as a thickener, emollient, and texture modifier. Creates a slight waxy feel and helps stabilize emulsions. Very mild; not a sensitizer.

name: Oryza Sativa Extract
explanation: Rice extract — contains ferulic acid and other antioxidants with brightening and soothing properties. Well-tolerated; not a sensitizer.

name: Ozokerite
explanation: A naturally occurring mineral wax used to structure anhydrous formulas. Not a sensitizer.

name: Palmitic Acid
explanation: A saturated fatty acid naturally found in the skin's own lipids and in many plant oils. Used as an emulsifier and skin conditioner in cosmetics. Skin-identical in origin and extremely well-tolerated — it's one of the building blocks of the skin barrier.

name: Palmitoyl Dipeptide-10
explanation: A lipopeptide anti-aging active. Well-tolerated.

name: Palmitoyl Hexapeptide-12
explanation: A lipopeptide used in anti-aging formulas to support skin firmness. Well-tolerated.

name: Palmitoyl Palmitamide Mea
explanation: A fatty acid amide used as an emollient and skin-conditioning agent. Well-tolerated.

name: Palmitoyl Pentapeptide-4
explanation: The peptide behind the Matrixyl name — a firmness anchor that signals the skin's repair process and boosts collagen and elastin production. It strengthens the extracellular matrix to make skin feel less fragile and raw over time. Well-tolerated, and the palmitoyl fatty acid attachment helps it reach where it needs to work.

name: Palmitoyl Pentapeptide-4
explanation: The peptide behind the Matrixyl name — a firmness anchor that signals the skin's repair process and boosts collagen and elastin production. It strengthens the extracellular matrix to make skin feel less fragile and raw over time. Well-tolerated, and the palmitoyl fatty acid attachment helps it reach where it needs to work.

name: Palmitoyl Tetrapeptide-7
explanation: One half of the Matrixyl 3000 complex — a firmness anchor peptide that targets the inflammatory side of skin aging and helps reduce collagen breakdown. It works alongside palmitoyl tripeptide-1 to address both new collagen production and existing collagen protection. Very well-tolerated.

name: Palmitoyl Tripeptide-1
explanation: The other half of Matrixyl 3000 — a firmness anchor peptide that mimics collagen fragments to stimulate the production of new collagen and hyaluronic acid (the skin's own kind, not topically applied). It strengthens the extracellular matrix from the inside, which is what makes skin feel less fragile with use. Well-tolerated for reactive skin.

name: Palmitoyl Tripeptide-1
explanation: The other half of Matrixyl 3000 — a firmness anchor peptide that mimics collagen fragments to stimulate the production of new collagen and hyaluronic acid (the skin's own kind, not topically applied). It strengthens the extracellular matrix from the inside, which is what makes skin feel less fragile with use. Well-tolerated for reactive skin.

name: Palmitoyl Tripeptide-37
explanation: A palmitic acid-linked tripeptide used in anti-aging formulas to support collagen and elastin. Well-tolerated; not a sensitizer.

name: Palmitoyl Tripeptide-8
explanation: A peptide firmness anchor with a specific focus on the skin's neurological sensitivity — it interferes with substance P, the compound that triggers the redness and hypersensitivity response in reactive skin. Strengthens the extracellular matrix while actively reducing the "everything hurts" sensitivity that characterizes reactive skin. One of the most relevant peptides for this skin type.

name: Panax Ginseng Berry Extract
explanation: Ginseng berry extract — rich in ginsenosides with antioxidant and skin-energizing properties. Well-tolerated.

name: Panax Ginseng Root Extract
explanation: Ginseng root extract — contains ginsenosides with antioxidant and mild anti-inflammatory activity. Used in anti-aging and brightening formulas. Well-tolerated; not a common sensitizer at cosmetic concentrations.

name: Panax Ginseng Seed Oil
explanation: Ginseng seed oil — lightweight, antioxidant-rich. Well-tolerated.

name: Pancratium Maritimum Extract
explanation: Sea daffodil bulb extract — antioxidant and brightening. Well-tolerated.

name: Panthenol
explanation: Provitamin B5 — converts to pantothenic acid in skin, where it supports barrier repair, wound healing, and hydration. Consistently recommended for sensitive and compromised skin because it genuinely accelerates barrier recovery without any meaningful irritation risk. One of the more straightforward "always safe" ingredients for reactive skin.

name: Panthenol(Skin-Conditioning Agents)
explanation: Same as panthenol — provitamin B5 with the functional category appended from the ingredient list format. Converts to pantothenic acid in skin to support barrier repair and hydration. Excellent tolerability; a useful ingredient for reactive skin to look for specifically.

name: Pantolactone
explanation: A panthenol-related compound with skin conditioning properties. Well-tolerated.

name: PEG-10 Dimethicone
explanation: A silicone-PEG hybrid that acts as an emulsifier in silicone-in-water formulas. Gives lightweight formulas a silky feel. Well-tolerated; not a sensitizer.

name: PEG-10 Isostearate
explanation: A PEG ester emulsifier and solubilizer. Well-tolerated at typical cosmetic use levels.

name: PEG-100 Stearate
explanation: A PEG ester emulsifier — a high-molecular-weight (100 units) form that functions primarily at the formula surface with minimal skin penetration. Well-tolerated; any concerns about PEGs in skincare relate to lower molecular weight forms, not PEG-100.

name: PEG-12 Dimethicone
explanation: A silicone-PEG hybrid that emulsifies silicones into water-based formulas. Gives products a light, slip-enhanced texture. Well-tolerated; not a sensitizer.

name: PEG-120 Methyl Glucose Dioleate
explanation: A PEG-modified glucose ester used as an emulsifier and thickener. Very gentle; often used in sensitive-skin cleansers.

name: PEG-150 Pentaerythrityl Tetrastearate
explanation: A high-MW PEG emulsifier used in stable formulas. Well-tolerated.

name: Peg-20
explanation: A polyethylene glycol used as a humectant and solvent. Well-tolerated.

name: PEG-20 Glyceryl Triisostearate
explanation: A PEG-based emulsifier and solubilizer used in cleansing oils and water-resistant formulas. Well-tolerated.

name: PEG-20 Sorbitan Isostearate
explanation: A gentle PEG sorbitan ester emulsifier. Well-tolerated.

name: PEG-200 Hydrogenated Glyceryl Palmate
explanation: A PEG-based emulsifier used in cleansing and conditioning formulas. The very high PEG chain (200 units) means minimal skin penetration. Well-tolerated.

name: PEG-240/HDI Copolymer Bis-Decyltetradeceth-20 Ether
explanation: A PEG-based polymeric emulsifier. Inert; not a sensitizer.

name: PEG-2M
explanation: A high-molecular-weight PEG used as a thickener. Inert; not a sensitizer.

name: PEG-30 Dipolyhydroxystearate
explanation: A PEG-based emulsifier used in mineral sunscreens and water-in-oil formulas. Well-tolerated.

name: PEG-30 Stearate
explanation: A PEG ester emulsifier. Well-tolerated.

name: PEG-4
explanation: A short-chain PEG used as a humectant and solvent. Well-tolerated.

name: PEG-4 Dilaurate
explanation: A PEG ester emulsifier and solubilizer. Well-tolerated.

name: PEG-4 Laurate
explanation: A PEG ester emulsifier. Well-tolerated.

name: PEG-40 Hydrogenated Castor Oil
explanation: A gentle, non-ionic emulsifier and solubilizer derived from hydrogenated castor oil. Used to solubilize fragrance and essential oils in water-based formulas. Well-tolerated.

name: PEG-40 Stearate
explanation: A PEG ester emulsifier. Well-tolerated at typical cosmetic use levels.

name: PEG-6 Caprylic/Capric Glycerides
explanation: A gentle PEG-based emulsifier derived from caprylic/capric triglycerides. Well-tolerated.

name: PEG-60 Almond Glycerides
explanation: A PEG-modified sweet almond oil derivative used as an emulsifier and solubilizer. Gentle; compatible with sensitive-skin formulas.

name: PEG-60 Hydrogenated Castor Oil
explanation: A gentle emulsifier and solubilizer derived from hydrogenated castor oil. Used to disperse oils and fragrance in water-based formulas. Well-tolerated; not a sensitizer.

name: PEG-7
explanation: A short-chain PEG used as a humectant and solvent. Well-tolerated at typical cosmetic use levels.

name: PEG-7 Glyceryl Cocoate
explanation: A gentle PEG-based emulsifier and surfactant derived from coconut oil and glycerin. Used in mild cleansing formulas. Well-tolerated; not a sensitizer.

name: PEG-9
explanation: A polyethylene glycol used as a humectant and solvent. Well-tolerated.

name: PEG-9 Polydimethylsiloxyethyl Dimethicone
explanation: A silicone-PEG emulsifier used to create water-in-silicone formulas with a light finish. Well-tolerated; not a sensitizer.

name: PEG/PPG-17/6 Copolymer
explanation: A PEG/PPG copolymer used as an emulsifier and solubilizer. Well-tolerated.

name: PEG/PPG-18/18 Dimethicone
explanation: A silicone-PEG/PPG hybrid emulsifier. Well-tolerated.

name: Pentaerythrityl Stearate/Caprate/Caprylate/Adipate
explanation: A complex emollient ester blend used in lip products and foundations. Not a sensitizer.

name: Pentaerythrityl Tetra-Di-T-Butyl Hydroxyhydrocinnamate
explanation: A hindered phenol antioxidant used to stabilize oils and other ingredients in formulas against oxidation. Present at trace concentrations; not a sensitizer.

name: Pentaerythrityl ­Tetra-­Di-­T-­Butyl ­Hydroxyhydrocinnamate
explanation: Antioxidant stabilizer for formula oils. Trace concentrations; not a sensitizer.

name: Pentaerythrityl Tetraethylhexanoate
explanation: A lightweight emollient ester that gives formulas a dry, non-greasy skin feel. Used in foundations and BB creams. Well-tolerated; not a sensitizer.

name: Pentaerythrityl Tetraisostearate
explanation: An emollient ester that gives formulas a rich yet non-greasy skin feel. Used in lip products and long-wear makeup. Well-tolerated; not a sensitizer.

name: Pentylene Glycol
explanation: A glycol used as a humectant, solvent, and mild antimicrobial — functionally similar to propanediol, often used in the same preservation systems. Less irritating than propylene glycol. Very well-tolerated; frequently found in formulas specifically designed for sensitive skin.

name: Perlite
explanation: A volcanic glass-derived mineral used as an absorbent and texture-lightening agent in cosmetics. Inert, non-sensitizing, and non-comedogenic. Often used in mineral makeup and mattifying formulas.

name: Persea Gratissima Oil
explanation: Avocado oil — rich in oleic acid, vitamins A, D, and E. A nourishing, penetrating emollient. Well-tolerated; not a sensitizer (avocado oil sensitivity is very rare and distinct from latex-fruit syndrome).

name: Phaseolus Angularis Seed Powder
explanation: Adzuki bean powder — a gentle physical exfoliant. Well-tolerated.

name: Phaseolus Radiatus Seed Extract
explanation: Mung bean seed extract — brightening and antioxidant. Well-tolerated.

name: Phenoxyethanol
explanation: The most widely used cosmetic preservative — effective against bacteria, yeast, and mold at low concentrations (typically ≤1%). Well-tolerated by the vast majority of skin types. Recommended by the EU Scientific Committee as an alternative to parabens. Not a practical sensitization concern for most people.

name: Phenyl Trimethicone
explanation: A silicone fluid that adds gloss and slip to formulas while enhancing the spreadability of makeup products. More refractive than standard dimethicone, lending a natural-finish effect. Well-tolerated; not a sensitizer.

name: Phenylalanine
explanation: An essential amino acid used in cosmetics for conditioning. Well-tolerated.

name: Phenyltrimethicone
explanation: A phenyl silicone that adds gloss and natural-finish appearance to formulas. Well-tolerated.

name: Phytonadione (Vitamin K)
explanation: Vitamin K1 — used topically to help reduce dark circles, bruising, and redness. Well-tolerated.

name: Phytosphingosine
explanation: A sphingolipid naturally found in the skin — a precursor to ceramides and an active component of the skin barrier's antimicrobial defense. Has documented antibacterial activity against acne bacteria and helps restore barrier integrity in reactive skin. Skin-identical, extremely well-tolerated, and specifically researched for sensitive and atopic skin.

name: Phytosterols
explanation: Plant sterols — structurally similar to cholesterol, supporting barrier repair. Very well-tolerated.

name: Phytosteryl Oleate
explanation: An ester of phytosterols and oleic acid used as an emollient. Well-tolerated.

name: Phytosteryl/Isostearyl/Cetyl/Stearyl/Behenyl Dimer Dilinoleate
explanation: A complex polyester emollient blend used in lip products. Not a sensitizer.

name: Pinus Palustris Leaf Extract
explanation: Longleaf pine leaf extract — contains proanthocyanidins and other antioxidants. Used in soothing and antioxidant formulas. Well-tolerated.

name: Pinus Pinaster Bark Extract
explanation: Pycnogenol / maritime pine bark extract — very potent antioxidant (OPCs). Well-tolerated.

name: Poly C10-30 Alkyl Acrylate
explanation: A synthetic polymer thickener similar to C10-30 alkyl acrylate crosspolymer. Not a sensitizer.

name: Polyacrylate Crosspolymer-6
explanation: A synthetic thickener and emulsion stabilizer used in gel and cream formulas. Not a sensitizer.

name: Polydiethyleneglycol Adipate/Ipdi Copolymer
explanation: A polyurethane-based film former used in long-wear makeup. Inert; not a sensitizer.

name: Polydimethylsiloxane/Polymethysilesquioxane Copolymer
explanation: A hybrid silicone copolymer for smooth texture in makeup. Inert; not a sensitizer.

name: Polyethylene
explanation: A synthetic wax or powder used as a texture modifier and thickener in lip products and makeup. Inert and non-sensitizing. Environmental concern as a microplastic in rinse-off products, but skin safety is not an issue.

name: Polyethylene Glycol
explanation: A polymer of ethylene oxide used as a humectant and solvent. Concern about penetration enhancers applies to low-MW forms; higher-MW versions stay on the skin surface. Generally well-tolerated.

name: Polyglyceryl 3-Hydroxylauryl Ether
explanation: A gentle polyglycerol-based surfactant. Well-tolerated.

name: Polyglyceryl-10 Laurate
explanation: A gentle polyglycerol ester emulsifier derived from lauric acid. The polyglycerol ester family has a very low irritation profile. No sensitization concerns.

name: Polyglyceryl-10 Myristate
explanation: A gentle polyglycerol-based emulsifier derived from myristic acid. The polyglycerol ester family is favored in sensitive-skin formulas for its very low irritation profile. No sensitization concerns.

name: Polyglyceryl-10 Stearate
explanation: A gentle emulsifier made from polyglycerol and stearic acid. The polyglycerol ester emulsifier family is favored in sensitive-skin formulas because of their very low irritation profile. No sensitization concerns.

name: Polyglyceryl-2 Dipolyhydroxystearate
explanation: A gentle polyglycerol ester emulsifier used in mineral sunscreens. Well-tolerated.

name: Polyglyceryl-2 Triisostearate
explanation: A polyglycerol-based emulsifier used in water-in-oil formulas. Gentle; well-tolerated.

name: Polyglyceryl-3 Diisostearate
explanation: A gentle polyglycerol-based emulsifier used in anhydrous and water-in-oil formulas. The polyglycerol ester family has a very low irritation profile. No sensitization concerns.

name: POLYGLYCERYL-3 DIISOSTEARATE 7743
explanation: A gentle polyglycerol ester emulsifier. No sensitization concerns.

name: Polyglyceryl-3 Methylglucose Distearate
explanation: A gentle emulsifier made from polyglycerol, methyl glucose, and stearic acid. Used in natural and sensitive-skin formulas. Low irritation profile; no sensitization concerns.

name: Polyglyceryl-3 Polydimethylsiloxyethyl Dimethicone
explanation: A silicone-based emulsifier used to create water-in-silicone emulsions with a light, non-greasy feel. Well-tolerated; not a sensitizer.

name: Polyglyceryl-3 Polyricinoleate
explanation: A polyglycerol-based emulsifier used in water-in-oil formulas. Gentle; well-tolerated.

name: Polyglyceryl-4 Caprate
explanation: A gentle polyglycerol ester surfactant and emulsifier. Well-tolerated.

name: Polyglyceryl-4 Isostearate
explanation: A gentle polyglycerol-based emulsifier used in mineral makeup and water-in-oil formulas. The polyglycerol ester family has a very low irritation profile. No sensitization concerns.

name: Polyglyceryl-­4 ­Isostearate
explanation: Gentle polyglycerol ester emulsifier. No sensitization concerns.

name: Polyglyceryl-5 Stearate
explanation: A gentle polyglycerol ester emulsifier. Well-tolerated.

name: Polygonum Cuspidatum Root Extract
explanation: Japanese knotweed extract — the primary natural source of resveratrol. Antioxidant. Well-tolerated.

name: Polygonum Multiflorum Extract
explanation: He shou wu extract — antioxidant and conditioning; used in anti-aging formulas. Well-tolerated topically.

name: Polyhydroxystearic Acid
explanation: A polymer emulsifier used in sunscreens to disperse mineral filters. Not a sensitizer.

name: Polymethyl Methacrylate
explanation: PMMA — spherical polymer particles used for a smooth, soft-focus effect in makeup. Inert; not a sensitizer.

name: Polymethylsilsesquioxane
explanation: A silicone-based spherical powder used for its soft-focus, pore-blurring optical effect in primers and foundations. Inert and non-sensitizing; not absorbed into skin.

name: Polyoxyl 40 Stearate
explanation: A PEG-40 stearate emulsifier and solubilizer. Well-tolerated at typical cosmetic use levels.

name: Polyphenylsilsesquioxane
explanation: A silicone resin used to add gloss and wear to lip products. Inert; not a sensitizer.

name: Polyquaternium-10
explanation: A cellulose-derived conditioning polymer that forms a smooth film on skin and hair. Very well-tolerated.

name: Polyquaternium-11
explanation: A cationic polymer used as a conditioning and film-forming agent. Provides a smooth skin feel. Well-tolerated; not a significant sensitizer at typical cosmetic concentrations.

name: Polyquaternium-51
explanation: A synthetic polymer that mimics the phospholipid structure of cell membranes (also called MBPC). Forms a moisture-retaining film on skin and improves texture. Very well-tolerated.

name: Polyquaternium-67
explanation: A cationic polymer used for conditioning and film-forming. Well-tolerated.

name: Polyquaternium-7
explanation: A cationic polymer used as a conditioning and film-forming agent. Provides a smooth, slip-enhanced skin feel. Well-tolerated; not a sensitizer at typical cosmetic concentrations.

name: Polysorbate 20
explanation: A very gentle emulsifier and solubilizer made from sorbitol and lauric acid. Widely used in sensitive-skin formulas and eye products because of its exceptionally low irritation profile. No sensitization concerns.

name: Polysorbate 60
explanation: A PEG-based emulsifier closely related to Polysorbate 80. Well-tolerated.

name: Polysorbate 80
explanation: A gentle, non-ionic emulsifier and solubilizer derived from sorbitol and oleic acid. Very widely used; well-tolerated.

name: Portulaca Oleracea Extract
explanation: Purslane extract — a soothing, antioxidant-rich plant extract with anti-inflammatory and barrier-supporting properties. Used in formulas targeting reactive, redness-prone skin. Well-tolerated.

name: Potassium Azeloyl Diglycinate
explanation: A water-soluble azelaic acid derivative with brightening, anti-inflammatory, and mild anti-bacterial properties. Well-tolerated even by reactive skin; gentler than azelaic acid itself.

name: Potassium Cetyl Phosphate
explanation: A mild emulsifier made from cetyl alcohol and phosphate. Gentle and compatible with sensitive-skin formulas. Well-tolerated with no known sensitization concerns.

name: Potassium Hydroxide
explanation: A strong alkali used in trace amounts to adjust formula pH — similar function to sodium hydroxide, but often preferred in formulas for its feel. Fully neutralized in the finished product.

name: Potassium Phosphate
explanation: A phosphate salt used as a pH buffer in cosmetic formulas. No skin-active properties; purely a stability and pH-management ingredient. Not a sensitizer.

name: Potassium Sorbate
explanation: A mild preservative (potassium salt of sorbic acid) used in food and cosmetics. Effective primarily against mold and yeast. Very gentle; not a sensitizer at typical cosmetic use levels. Often used in combination with sodium benzoate.

name: PPG-10 Methyl Glucose Ether
explanation: A PPG-modified glucose derivative used as a humectant and skin conditioner. Well-tolerated.

name: PPG-13-Decyltetradeceth-24
explanation: A PPG/PEG-based emulsifier used in silicone-containing formulas to create stable emulsions. Well-tolerated; not a sensitizer.

name: PPG-26-Buteth-26
explanation: A PPG/PEG-based emulsifier and solubilizer. Well-tolerated.

name: PPG-5-Ceteth-20
explanation: A PEG/PPG-based emulsifier and solubilizer used to disperse oils in water-based formulas. Well-tolerated; not a sensitizer.

name: Proline
explanation: An amino acid used in cosmetics as a skin-conditioning agent. A key component of collagen. Very well-tolerated.

name: Propanediol
explanation: A plant-derived (corn-sourced) glycol used as a humectant, solvent, and penetration enhancer. Widely regarded as gentler than propylene glycol — less likely to irritate sensitive or reactive skin. Also improves the absorption of other ingredients in the formula. Well-tolerated.

name: Propolis
explanation: A resinous substance bees produce from plant saps to seal their hives, with genuine antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory properties that have been studied for acne and wound healing. Well-tolerated for this skin. People with bee product allergies (beeswax, honey) should patch test, but absent that history, propolis is a safe and useful ingredient.

name: Propolis Extract
explanation: Concentrated bee propolis standardized for its active phenolic compounds. Has antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory activity, with some evidence for acne applications. Well-tolerated for this skin. Same caveat as raw propolis: known bee product allergies warrant a patch test first.

name: Propylene Carbonate
explanation: A cyclic carbonate used as a solvent in mineral makeup dispersions. Well-tolerated.

name: Propylene Glycol
explanation: A humectant and solvent widely used in cosmetics. Well-tolerated by most skin types. Occasionally reported as a mild irritant at high concentrations on very sensitive skin, but at typical cosmetic use levels (usually <5%) it's not a practical concern.

name: Propylene Glycol Laurate
explanation: An emollient ester of propylene glycol and lauric acid. Gives formulas a light, non-greasy feel. Well-tolerated.

name: Prunus Amygdalus Dulcis Oil
explanation: Sweet almond oil — a lightweight, balanced emollient rich in oleic and linoleic acids. Very well-tolerated.

name: Prunus Amygdalus Dulcis Seed Extract
explanation: Sweet almond seed extract — conditioning, with mild emollient properties. Well-tolerated.

name: Prunus Armeniaca (Apricot) Fruit Extract
explanation: Apricot fruit extract — antioxidant-rich. Well-tolerated.

name: Prunus Armeniaca Seed Powder
explanation: Apricot seed powder used as a gentle physical exfoliant in scrubs. At fine-milled particle sizes, it is less abrasive than nut shells.

name: Prunus Mume Fruit Extract
explanation: Japanese plum (ume) fruit extract — contains citric acid and other organic acids with antioxidant properties. At extract concentrations, not a practical exfoliant. Well-tolerated.

name: Pteris Multifida Extract
explanation: Chinese brake fern extract — antioxidant. Well-tolerated.

name: Pueraria Lobata Root Extract
explanation: Kudzu root extract — contains puerarin with antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties. Well-tolerated.

name: Pulsatilla Koreana Extract
explanation: Korean pasqueflower extract — calming and anti-inflammatory. Used in soothing Korean skincare formulas. Well-tolerated.

name: Pumice
explanation: A volcanic rock used as a gentle physical exfoliant in scrubs. Inert; not a sensitizer.

name: Punica Granatum Extract
explanation: Pomegranate extract — rich in ellagic acid and anthocyanins with antioxidant properties. Well-tolerated.

name: Purified Water
explanation: Deionized/purified water used as a formula base. Inert.

name: Pvm/Ma Copolymer
explanation: A polyvinyl methyl ether/maleic acid copolymer used as a film former and adhesive in makeup. Inert; not a sensitizer.

name: PVP
explanation: Polyvinylpyrrolidone — a film former used in makeup and sunscreens to improve adhesion. Inert; not a sensitizer.

name: Pyrus Malus (Apple) Fruit Extract
explanation: Apple fruit extract — contains polyphenols and malic acid. Antioxidant. Well-tolerated.

name: Pyrus Malus Fruit Extract
explanation: Apple fruit extract — contains malic acid and polyphenols. At extract concentrations in formulas, malic acid content is too low for meaningful exfoliating activity. Used for its antioxidant and conditioning properties. Well-tolerated.

name: Pyrus' Malus (Apple) Fruit Extract
explanation: Apple fruit extract (typographic variant) — same ingredient as Pyrus Malus fruit extract. Well-tolerated.

name: Raspberry Isolates
explanation: Isolated compounds from raspberry — typically fragrance or antioxidant components. Well-tolerated.

name: Red 6
explanation: D&C Red 6 — a synthetic lake dye used in lip products. Approved for cosmetic use. Inert.

name: Red 7
explanation: D&C Red 7 — a synthetic lake dye used in lip products. Approved for cosmetic use. Inert.

name: Red 7 Ruby: Diisostearyl Malate
explanation: Diisostearyl malate — emollient for lip products. Well-tolerated.

name: Resveratrol
explanation: A polyphenol antioxidant from grapes and Japanese knotweed. Anti-aging and antioxidant activity. Very well-tolerated.

name: Rh-Oligopeptide-1
explanation: A recombinant human EGF-like peptide that may support skin repair and renewal. Well-tolerated; not a sensitizer.

name: Riboflavin
explanation: Vitamin B2 — used as an antioxidant and natural yellow pigment in cosmetics. Very well-tolerated.

name: Rice Ferment Filtrate
explanation: Fermented rice water — contains amino acids, vitamins, and ferulic acid. Brightening and conditioning. Well-tolerated.

name: Ricinus Communis Seed Oil
explanation: Castor oil — a thick, viscous oil rich in ricinoleic acid with humectant and film-forming properties. Commonly used in lip products. Well-tolerated; not a sensitizer in typical cosmetic use.

name: Rosa Canina Fruit Oil
explanation: Rosehip oil — naturally high in retinoids (trans-retinoic acid) and linoleic acid. Supports barrier repair and has mild anti-aging benefits. Well-tolerated; the natural retinoid content is low enough not to cause the irritation of concentrated retinol.

name: Rosa Canina Fruit Oil*
explanation: Rosehip oil — high in linoleic acid and natural retinoids. Well-tolerated.

name: Rosa Damascena Flower Extract
explanation: Damask rose flower extract — contains polyphenols with antioxidant and soothing properties. Distinct from rose essential oil; much lower sensitization risk at extract concentrations. Well-tolerated.

name: Rosa Gallica Flower Extract
explanation: French rose flower extract — contains polyphenols with antioxidant and soothing properties. Well-tolerated.

name: Rosa Rugosa Flower Extract
explanation: Japanese rose flower extract — antioxidant and soothing. Well-tolerated.

name: Rosmarinus Officinalis Leaf Extract
explanation: Rosemary leaf extract — distinct from rosemary essential oil. The extract contains rosmarinic acid and carnosic acid with potent antioxidant activity. Much lower sensitization risk than the oil. Well-tolerated at cosmetic concentrations.

name: Royal Jelly Extract
explanation: Royal jelly — secreted by worker bees; contains 10-HDA, proteins, and vitamins. Skin-conditioning. Well-tolerated for most; those with bee/honey allergy should patch-test.

name: Rubus Idaeus Fruit Extract
explanation: Raspberry fruit extract — antioxidant-rich. Well-tolerated.

name: Saccharomyces Ferment
explanation: A yeast ferment that produces beneficial metabolites including vitamins, amino acids, and antimicrobial peptides. Well-tolerated.

name: Saccharomyces Ferment Filtrate
explanation: The filtered extract of yeast fermentation — rich in amino acids, vitamins (especially B vitamins), and beta-glucan. Conditioning and brightening. Well-tolerated.

name: Saccharum Officinarum Extract
explanation: Sugarcane extract — a source of glycolic acid at low concentrations, but also contains polysaccharides, amino acids, and other conditioning components. In extract form, the AHA content is typically too low to cause irritation. Well-tolerated.

name: Sage (Salvia Officinalis) Extract
explanation: Sage extract (common name format). Antioxidant and antimicrobial. Well-tolerated at extract concentrations.

name: Sage Extract
explanation: Sage leaf extract — distinct from sage essential oil. The extract has antioxidant and antimicrobial properties without the camphor and thujone sensitization risk of the oil. Well-tolerated at cosmetic concentrations.

name: Salvia Officinalis Leaf Extract
explanation: Sage leaf extract — antioxidant and antimicrobial. Distinct from sage essential oil (which is flagged). Well-tolerated.

name: Saponaria Officinalis Leaf Extract
explanation: Soapwort extract — contains saponins with mild cleansing and soothing properties. Well-tolerated at extract concentrations.

name: Sapphire Powder
explanation: Fine-ground sapphire (aluminum oxide) used as a luxury skin-feel ingredient and mild exfoliant. Inert; not a sensitizer.

name: Scutellaria Baicalensis Root Extract
explanation: Baical skullcap root extract — contains baicalin and other flavonoids with potent antioxidant and anti-inflammatory activity. Widely used in Korean and Japanese skincare for reactive and redness-prone skin. Well-tolerated.

name: Sd Alcohol 40
explanation: Specially denatured ethanol (SD 40) used in cosmetics as a solvent, antimicrobial, and astringent. At typical concentrations in leave-on products it may be slightly drying, but in serums and toners it evaporates quickly. Not a sensitizer; the drying effect is the main concern for very dry reactive skin.

name: SEBACIC ACID
explanation: A dicarboxylic acid used in barrier-repair formulas. Occurs naturally in sebum. Well-tolerated.

name: Serica Soie
explanation: Silk protein — conditioning with a silky skin feel. Well-tolerated.

name: Serica Soie)
explanation: Silk protein or powder — conditioning with a silky skin feel. Well-tolerated.

name: Sesamum Indicum Seed Extract
explanation: Sesame seed extract — contains sesamin and other antioxidants. Well-tolerated; sesame seed allergy is possible but rare via topical application.

name: Silica
explanation: Silicon dioxide — used in cosmetics as an absorbent, anti-caking agent, and texture modifier. Gives makeup a soft-focus, pore-blurring effect. Inert and non-sensitizing; not absorbed into skin.

name: Silica Dimethyl Silylate
explanation: Surface-modified silica used as an absorbent and thickener in anhydrous formulas. Inert; not a sensitizer.

name: Silica Silylate
explanation: Silyl-modified silica used as a thickener and absorbent in anhydrous formulas. Inert; not a sensitizer.

name: Silk Powder
explanation: Finely ground silk protein used in makeup for a smooth, soft-focus effect. Well-tolerated.

name: Simethicone
explanation: A silicone anti-foaming and skin-protective agent. Used in diaper creams and wound care. Very well-tolerated; approved for medical use.

name: Simmondsia Chinensis Seed Oil
explanation: Jojoba — technically a liquid wax (not a triglyceride oil), making it uniquely stable and structurally similar to skin's own sebum. This similarity means it absorbs readily without clogging pores. One of the most reliable emollients for reactive skin: non-comedogenic, shelf-stable, and consistently well-tolerated.

name: Sodium Acrylate/Sodium Acryloyldimethyl Taurate Copolymer
explanation: A synthetic polymer thickener used to create gel textures. Well-tolerated.

name: Sodium Alginate
explanation: Seaweed-derived thickener and film former. Very gentle; used in face masks. Well-tolerated.

name: Sodium Benzoate
explanation: A mild preservative (sodium salt of benzoic acid) widely used in both food and cosmetics. Effective against yeast and bacteria at low concentrations. Generally well-tolerated in skincare; much gentler than formaldehyde-releasing preservatives. Not a practical concern at typical cosmetic use levels.

name: Sodium ­Benzoate
explanation: Mild preservative. Well-tolerated.

name: Sodium Chloride
explanation: Salt — used in cosmetics to adjust viscosity and tonicity of aqueous formulas. Physiologically identical to the salt in skin cells. No sensitization concerns whatsoever at typical cosmetic use levels.

name: Sodium Citrate
explanation: The sodium salt of citric acid used as a pH buffer and chelating agent. Helps maintain formula stability and keeps pH at the skin-friendly range. No skin-active properties at these concentrations; not a sensitizer.

name: Sodium Cocoamphoacetate
explanation: A mild amphoteric surfactant derived from coconut oil. Gentle and conditioning; suitable for sensitive-skin cleansers.

name: Sodium Cocoyl Alaninate
explanation: An amino acid-based (alanine) mild surfactant. Gentle and skin-compatible; suitable for sensitive-skin cleansers.

name: Sodium Cocoyl Glycinate
explanation: An amino acid-based (glycine) mild surfactant derived from coconut. Gentle and skin-compatible.

name: Sodium Cocoyl Isethionate
explanation: A very gentle isethionate surfactant derived from coconut. Used in syndet bars and sensitive-skin cleansers. Well-tolerated.

name: Sodium Hydrolyzed Potato Starch Dodecenylsuccinate
explanation: A modified starch used as an emulsifier and texture agent. Well-tolerated.

name: Sodium Hydroxide
explanation: A strong alkali used in trace amounts to adjust formula pH — neutralizes carbomers, sets acidity to the correct range, and creates the environment where other ingredients function. Fully neutralized in the finished product; no residual caustic activity. Seeing it on an ingredient list doesn't mean the product is harsh or alkaline.

name: Sodium Isethionate
explanation: An isethionate-derived mild surfactant. Gentle and skin-compatible.

name: Sodium Lactate
explanation: The sodium salt of lactic acid — used as a humectant and mild pH adjuster. Unlike free lactic acid, it has minimal exfoliating activity at typical concentrations. Part of the skin's NMF. Well-tolerated.

name: Sodium Lauroyl Lactylate
explanation: A gentle emulsifier and mild surfactant made from lauric acid and lactic acid — biodegradable and often used in sensitive-skin and baby products. Extremely well-tolerated.

name: Sodium Lauroyl Methyl Isethionate
explanation: A mild isethionate-based surfactant with excellent skin feel and low irritation. One of the gentler cleansing agents available — used in sensitive-skin and baby cleansers. Well-tolerated.

name: Sodium Lauroyl Sarcosinate
explanation: An amino acid-based (sarcosine) mild surfactant used in cleansers. Gentler than sulfates, with good skin feel and low irritation. Well-tolerated even by reactive skin.

name: Sodium Laurylglucosides Hydroxypropylsulfonate
explanation: A mild glucose-derived surfactant with gentle cleansing properties. Well-tolerated.

name: Sodium Methyl Cocoyl Taurate
explanation: A taurine-derived mild surfactant. Gentle with good skin feel; suitable for sensitive-skin formulas.

name: Sodium Palmitoyl Proline
explanation: An amino acid-derived film former and conditioning agent. Well-tolerated.

name: Sodium PCA
explanation: The sodium salt of pyrrolidone carboxylic acid — a key component of the skin's natural moisturizing factor (NMF). Naturally present in healthy skin and highly effective at attracting moisture. One of the most skin-compatible humectants available.

name: Sodium Phytate
explanation: A chelating agent derived from phytic acid (found in plants) — a greener, biodegradable alternative to EDTA. Binds metal ions to stabilize formulas. Not a sensitizer.

name: Sodium Polyacrylate
explanation: A crosslinked polyacrylate polymer used as a thickener and absorbent in gels and creams. The same technology used in medical wound dressings. Creates smooth gel textures and absorbs excess moisture. Very well-tolerated and non-sensitizing.

name: Sodium Polyacryloyldimethyl Taurate
explanation: A synthetic polymer thickener and emulsifier. Well-tolerated.

name: Sodium Sulfate
explanation: A mineral salt used as a tonicity agent and processing aid. Not a sensitizer.

name: Sodium Surfactin
explanation: A biosurfactant produced by Bacillus subtilis fermentation. Very mild with antimicrobial and skin-moisturizing properties. Well-tolerated.

name: Solanum Melongena Fruit Extract
explanation: Eggplant fruit extract — contains anthocyanins with antioxidant properties. Used in brightening and antioxidant formulas. Well-tolerated; not a sensitizer.

name: Sorbeth-30 Tetraoleate
explanation: A hydrophilic emulsifier and solubilizer used in cleansing oils — the ingredient that causes the formula to emulsify into a milky rinse on contact with water. Essential for rinse-off cleansing oil performance. Very gentle; no known skin sensitization concerns.

name: Sorbic Acid
explanation: A naturally occurring fatty acid and mild preservative effective primarily against mold and yeast. The same compound as potassium sorbate (the salt form). Very well-tolerated at typical cosmetic use levels.

name: Sorbitan Isostearate
explanation: An emulsifier derived from sorbitol and isostearic acid that stabilizes water-in-oil emulsions. Gentle; no known sensitization concerns.

name: Sorbitan Laurate
explanation: A gentle sorbitan ester emulsifier from sorbitol and lauric acid. Well-tolerated.

name: Sorbitan Oleate
explanation: A sorbitan-based emulsifier derived from oleic acid. Well-tolerated.

name: Sorbitan Olivate
explanation: An emulsifier derived from sorbitol and olive oil fatty acids, used in natural and sensitive-skin formulas to stabilize water-in-oil emulsions. Very gentle; not a sensitizer.

name: Sorbitan Sesquioleate
explanation: An emulsifier derived from sorbitol and oleic acid that stabilizes water-in-oil emulsions. Mild and well-tolerated — often chosen for formulas targeting sensitive skin because of its low irritation profile.

name: Sorbitan Stearate
explanation: An emulsifier derived from sorbitol and stearic acid. Gentle and compatible with sensitive-skin formulas. No sensitization concerns.

name: Sorbitan Tristearate
explanation: A sorbitan ester emulsifier used to stabilize water-in-oil emulsions. Not a sensitizer.

name: Sorbitol
explanation: A sugar alcohol humectant derived from glucose. Widely used in food and cosmetics to draw moisture to the skin. Very well-tolerated; not a sensitizer.

name: Sparassis Crispa Extract
explanation: Cauliflower mushroom extract — contains beta-glucans with soothing properties. Well-tolerated.

name: Squalane
explanation: A stable, plant-derived (usually olive or sugarcane) form of squalene that closely mimics the skin's own natural oils. Lightweight, non-comedogenic, and one of the most universally well-tolerated emollients available. Reactive skin tends to respond very well — it reinforces the barrier without occlusion, without fragrance, and without any of the common irritation triggers.

name: Squalane(Skin-Conditioning Agents)
explanation: Same as squalane — the category label is appended from the ingredient list format. A stable, plant-derived emollient that mimics the skin's own lipids: lightweight, non-comedogenic, and one of the safest emollient choices for reactive skin.

name: Stearalkonium Hectorite
explanation: A clay-based thickener and suspending agent for anhydrous formulas. Inert; not a sensitizer.

name: Stearic Acid
explanation: A saturated fatty acid naturally found in skin and in many plant fats. Used as an emulsifier, thickener, and skin conditioner. Very well-tolerated — skin-identical and non-sensitizing.

name: Stearyl Alcohol
explanation: A C18 fatty alcohol used as an emollient and thickener. Gentle; widely used in sensitive-skin formulas.

name: Stearyl Glycyrrhetinate
explanation: A licorice derivative (stearyl ester of glycyrrhetinic acid) with potent anti-inflammatory properties. Better skin adhesion than the parent compound. Very well-tolerated; favored for reactive and redness-prone skin.

name: Styrene/Acrylates Copolymer
explanation: A synthetic polymer used as a film former and binder in long-wear makeup. Helps products adhere and resist transfer. Inert on skin; not a sensitizer.

name: Styrene/Vp Copolymer
explanation: A film-forming polymer used to improve adhesion of makeup. Inert; not a sensitizer.

name: Sucralose
explanation: A synthetic sweetener used in cosmetics as a humectant or taste-masking agent in lip products. Inert; not a sensitizer.

name: Sucrose Cocoate
explanation: A sugar-derived emulsifier from sucrose and coconut fatty acids. Gentle and biodegradable. Well-tolerated.

name: Sucrose Distearate
explanation: A gentle sugar-based emulsifier made from sucrose and stearic acid. Biodegradable and well-tolerated.

name: Sucrose Stearate
explanation: An emulsifier made from sugar and stearic acid — gentle, biodegradable, and derived from plant sources. Used in sensitive-skin formulas as a very mild surfactant and emulsifier. No sensitization concerns.

name: Synthetic Beeswax
explanation: A synthetic alternative to natural beeswax — avoids allergy concerns associated with propolis in natural beeswax. Well-tolerated.

name: Synthetic Fluorphlogopite
explanation: Synthetic mica — laboratory-made mica free from the mining concerns of natural mica. Provides the same shimmer and opacity. Inert and non-sensitizing.

name: Synthetic Wax
explanation: A general term for polyethylene or Fischer-Tropsch waxes used as thickeners and texture modifiers in makeup. Inert and non-sensitizing.

name: T-Butyl Alcohol
explanation: A branched alcohol used as a solvent and processing aid. Typically evaporates before product reaches the consumer. Well-tolerated at trace levels.

name: Talc
explanation: A mineral used in pressed powder, eyeshadow, and blush as an absorbent and anti-caking agent. Cosmetic-grade talc is tested to be free of asbestos contamination (the historical concern). Not a sensitizer; inert on skin.

name: Taurine
explanation: A sulfonic amino acid used in cosmetics for its osmoprotective properties and mild skin-conditioning effects. Very well-tolerated.

name: Terminalia Ferdinandiana Fruit Extract
explanation: Kakadu plum extract — the highest natural source of Vitamin C of any fruit. Antioxidant and brightening. Well-tolerated.

name: Tetrasodium EDTA
explanation: The tetrasodium salt of EDTA — a chelating agent that binds metal ions to stabilize formulas. Functionally identical to disodium EDTA. No sensitization concerns at typical cosmetic use levels.

name: Tetrasodium Glutamate Diacetate
explanation: A biodegradable chelating agent derived from glutamic acid — an EDTA alternative. Binds metal ions to stabilize formulas. Not a sensitizer.

name: Theobroma Cacao Butter
explanation: Cocoa butter — a rich, occlusive emollient. Has a moderate comedogenicity rating. Well-tolerated by most reactive skin unless comedogenicity is a concern.

name: Theobroma Cacao Seed Extract
explanation: Cocoa seed extract — contains flavanols with antioxidant and mild anti-inflammatory properties. Well-tolerated.

name: Thioctic Acid
explanation: Alpha-lipoic acid — a potent antioxidant active in both water and oil phases. May cause mild tingling in some; at typical concentrations in cosmetics, generally well-tolerated.

name: Threonine
explanation: An essential amino acid used in cosmetics for conditioning. Well-tolerated.

name: Thymus Vulgaris Flower/Leaf/Stem Extract
explanation: Thyme extract — antimicrobial and antioxidant. Well-tolerated at extract concentrations.

name: Tin Oxide
explanation: An inorganic mineral used as a whitening pigment in nail products and some makeup. Inert; not a sensitizer.

name: Titanium Dioxide
explanation: Titanium dioxide — the gentler of the two mineral UV filters (alongside zinc oxide). Physically reflects and scatters UV light with no chemical reaction in skin. The gold standard for reactive skin: no itching, no stinging, no photosensitization. Also used as a white pigment in makeup.

name: Titanium Dioxide [Nano]
explanation: Nano-sized titanium dioxide — improves transparency while maintaining UV-blocking. Does not penetrate intact skin. Safe.

name: Titanium Dioxide [Nano] / Titanium Dioxide
explanation: Titanium dioxide — nano-sized particles improve transparency and feel in sunscreens while maintaining UV-blocking efficacy. Well-tolerated; nano-TiO2 does not penetrate intact skin.

name: Tocopherol
explanation: Vitamin E — a fat-soluble antioxidant that protects the skin barrier from free radical damage and helps stabilize other ingredients in the formula. Functions as both an antioxidant and a mild emollient. Extremely well-tolerated across skin types including reactive; rarely the cause of any reaction.

name: Tocopheryl Acetate
explanation: A stabilized ester form of Vitamin E — more shelf-stable than pure tocopherol. Converted to active tocopherol in skin. Protects against free radical damage and has mild emollient properties. Very well-tolerated.

name: Trametes Versicolor Extract
explanation: Turkey tail mushroom extract — polysaccharide-rich with antioxidant properties. Well-tolerated.

name: Trehalose
explanation: A disaccharide sugar that functions as a powerful humectant and cryo-protectant for skin cells. Found naturally in organisms that survive extreme dryness. Very well-tolerated.

name: Tribehenin
explanation: A triglyceride derived from behenic acid (a C22 fatty acid). Used as an emollient and structuring agent in lip products and rich creams. Well-tolerated; not a sensitizer.

name: Tricaprylin
explanation: A triglyceride of caprylic acid — lightweight, non-greasy emollient similar to caprylic/capric triglyceride. Well-tolerated.

name: Tridecane
explanation: A lightweight hydrocarbon emollient. Not a sensitizer.

name: Trideceth-10
explanation: A PEG-based emulsifier and solubilizer. Well-tolerated.

name: Trideceth-6
explanation: A PEG-based emulsifier and surfactant. Well-tolerated at typical cosmetic use levels.

name: Tridecyl Trimellitate
explanation: An ester emollient used in foundations for a smooth, long-wear texture. Not a sensitizer.

name: Triethoxycaprylyl Silane
explanation: Silane coating agent for mineral pigments. Trace concentrations; not a sensitizer.

name: Triethoxycaprylylsilane
explanation: A silane coupling agent used to coat mineral pigments (titanium dioxide, iron oxides) in sunscreens and makeup, improving water resistance and dispersibility. Present at trace quantities; not a sensitizer.

name: Triethyl Citrate
explanation: An ester of citric acid used as a plasticizer, emollient, and mild deodorant agent. At cosmetic concentrations it has no acidic exfoliating activity — it's been fully esterified. Well-tolerated and not a sensitizer.

name: Triethylene Glycol
explanation: A glycol used as a humectant and solvent. Well-tolerated.

name: Triethylhexanoin
explanation: A lightweight emollient ester that gives formulas a silky, non-greasy texture. Often used in anhydrous and silicone-based formulas. Well-tolerated; not a sensitizer.

name: Trihydroxystearin
explanation: A castor oil derivative used as a thickener and emollient in anhydrous formulas. Not a sensitizer.

name: Trimethylsiloxysilicate
explanation: A silicone resin used in long-wear makeup formulas to extend wear time and improve adhesion. Creates a flexible film on skin. Non-sensitizing; purely a performance ingredient.

name: Tripeptide-1
explanation: A three-amino-acid peptide (glycine-histidine-lysine) with copper-binding properties — the structural building block of the copper tripeptide GHK-Cu, even without the copper attached. It functions as a firmness anchor, supporting the extracellular matrix and wound-healing signaling. Well-tolerated, consistent with the peptide class.

name: Trisiloxane
explanation: A very lightweight volatile silicone used as a carrier. Evaporates quickly after application. Well-tolerated.

name: Trisodium Ethylenediamine Disuccinate
explanation: A chelating agent — binds metal ions in water and formulas that would otherwise degrade other ingredients over time. Functions as a greener alternative to EDTA. Purely a stability aid at trace concentrations; no skin-active properties and no sensitization concerns.

name: Triticum Vulgare Germ Oil
explanation: Wheat germ oil — very high in Vitamin E with emollient properties. Can be comedogenic at high concentrations. Well-tolerated for most.

name: Triticum Vulgare Seed Extract
explanation: Wheat seed extract — conditioning. Note: hydrolyzed wheat protein in rinse-off products has been linked to wheat sensitization in some individuals; seed extracts carry lower risk. Well-tolerated for most.

name: Tromethamine
explanation: An organic amine used to adjust formula pH — a common alternative to sodium hydroxide for neutralizing carbomers and acidic ingredients. Well-tolerated at typical cosmetic concentrations; not a sensitizer.

name: Ubiquinone
explanation: Coenzyme Q10 — a cellular antioxidant. Well-tolerated; used in anti-aging formulas.

name: Ulmus Davidiana Root Extract
explanation: David's elm root extract — used in Korean skincare for its soothing and skin-conditioning properties. Well-tolerated.

name: Undaria Pinnatifida Extract
explanation: Wakame seaweed extract — contains fucoidan and alginates with moisturizing and antioxidant properties. Well-tolerated.

name: Undecane
explanation: A lightweight hydrocarbon emollient that evaporates after application. Not a sensitizer.

name: Undecylenoyl Glycine
explanation: An amino acid-based antimicrobial agent derived from undecylenic acid. Well-tolerated.

name: Urea
explanation: At 2–5%, urea is a highly effective humectant and NMF component. At higher concentrations it becomes a keratolytic (exfoliant). Particularly beneficial for very dry, reactive, or eczema-prone skin. Well-tolerated at humectant concentrations.

name: Urtica Dioica (Nettle) Leaf Extract
explanation: Nettle leaf extract — anti-inflammatory and antioxidant. Well-tolerated at extract concentrations.

name: Urtica Dioica Leaf Extract
explanation: Stinging nettle leaf extract — despite the plant's sting (which comes from the fresh leaf hair, not the extract), the processed extract has anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties. Well-tolerated in cosmetics.

name: Usnea Barbata Extract
explanation: Old man's beard lichen extract — antimicrobial (usnic acid content). Well-tolerated at cosmetic concentrations.

name: Vaccinium Angustifolium Fruit Extract
explanation: Lowbush blueberry extract — rich in anthocyanins with antioxidant activity. Well-tolerated.

name: Vaccinium Macrocarpon (Cranberry) Fruit Extract
explanation: Cranberry fruit extract — rich in anthocyanins and vitamin C. Antioxidant. Well-tolerated.

name: Vaccinium Macrocarpon Fruit Juice*
explanation: Cranberry juice — contains antioxidant polyphenols. Well-tolerated.

name: Vaccinium Myrtillus Fruit Extract
explanation: Bilberry extract — rich in anthocyanins with antioxidant properties. Well-tolerated.

name: Valine
explanation: An essential amino acid used in cosmetics for conditioning. Well-tolerated.

name: Vanilla Isolates
explanation: Isolated compounds from vanilla — typically vanillin for antioxidant or fragrance use. Well-tolerated.

name: Vanilla Planifolia Fruit Extract
explanation: Vanilla extract — contains vanillin with antioxidant properties. Well-tolerated.

name: Vegetable Oil
explanation: A generic term for plant-derived oils used as emollients. The specific oil may vary by formulation; typically refers to common carrier oils like sunflower, soybean, or canola. Generally well-tolerated.

name: Vigna Radiata Seed Extract
explanation: Mung bean seed extract — antioxidant and conditioning. Well-tolerated.

name: Vinyl Dimethicone
explanation: A reactive silicone used to create crosslinked elastomers (silicone gels). Inert on skin; not a sensitizer.

name: Vinyl Dimethicone/Methicone Silsesquioxane Crosspolymer
explanation: A crosslinked silicone elastomer powder used for soft-focus and pore-blurring effects in makeup. Inert; not a sensitizer.

name: Vinylidene Chloride Copolymer
explanation: A copolymer used in multicomponent film-forming systems in makeup. Inert at cosmetic use levels.

name: Vitis Vinifera Fruit Cell Extract)
explanation: Grape stem cell extract — antioxidant. Well-tolerated.

name: Vitis Vinifera Fruit Extract
explanation: Grape fruit extract — contains resveratrol and other polyphenol antioxidants. Used in antioxidant and anti-aging formulas. Well-tolerated.

name: Vitis Vinifera Seed Oil
explanation: Grape seed oil — a lightweight, high-linoleic carrier oil pressed from grape seeds. Absorbs quickly, leaves a light skin feel, and has mild antioxidant activity from its proanthocyanidin content. Non-comedogenic at typical use levels. One of the lighter oils; a good choice for reactive skin that needs emollience without heaviness.

name: Vitreoscilla Ferment
explanation: A ferment derived from an aquatic bacterium that produces hemoglobin-like proteins. Used in cosmetics to improve skin oxygenation and barrier function. Well-tolerated; not a sensitizer.

name: VP/Hexadecene Copolymer
explanation: A synthetic film-forming polymer used to improve wear and adhesion of makeup. Inert; not a sensitizer.

name: Water
explanation: Water — the universal solvent base for water-phase skincare formulas. No skin concerns on its own; the surrounding ingredients dissolved in it are what the skin interacts with.

name: Xanthan Gum
explanation: A polysaccharide thickener produced by bacterial fermentation — widely used in food and skincare to create gel textures. Very well-tolerated; not a sensitizer. Found in everything from natural serums to prescription wound gels.

name: Xylitol
explanation: A sugar alcohol used as a humectant and mild antimicrobial in cosmetics. Identical to the food-grade sweetener; well-tolerated and not a sensitizer.

name: Xylitylglucoside
explanation: A sugar-based humectant (part of the aquaxyl complex with xylitol and anhydroxylitol) that helps strengthen the skin's water-holding capacity. Very well-tolerated.

name: Yeast Extract
explanation: A hydrolyzed yeast extract rich in amino acids and B vitamins. Conditioning and brightening. Well-tolerated.

name: Yeast Ferment Extract
explanation: Yeast ferment extract — rich in amino acids, vitamins, and beta-glucan. Conditioning. Well-tolerated.

name: Yellow 5
explanation: Tartrazine — a widely used synthetic yellow dye, approved by FDA and EU for cosmetics. Inert at typical use levels.

name: Yellow 6 Fleur: Diisostearyl Malate
explanation: Diisostearyl malate — an emollient used in lip products for a non-sticky gloss. Well-tolerated.

name: Yogurt Powder
explanation: Dehydrated yogurt — contains lactic acid and proteins. Mild exfoliating and conditioning. Well-tolerated.

name: Zanthoxylum Piperitum Fruit Extract
explanation: Sichuan pepper extract — mild antimicrobial and antioxidant properties. Well-tolerated at extract concentrations.

name: Zea Mays Kernel Extract
explanation: Corn kernel extract — conditioning and soothing. Well-tolerated.

name: Zea Mays Oil
explanation: Corn oil — a lightweight, linoleic-acid-rich carrier oil. Emollient and supportive of the skin barrier. Well-tolerated.

name: Zea Mays Starch
explanation: Corn starch used as an absorbent, texture modifier, and slip agent. Creates a soft, dry skin feel. Well-tolerated and non-irritating — essentially the same as the food ingredient applied topically.

name: Zea Mays Starch / Corn Starch
explanation: Corn starch — a soft powder used to absorb excess oil and improve skin feel. Inert; not a sensitizer.

name: Zinc
explanation: In skincare, zinc typically arrives as zinc oxide (mineral sunscreen and anti-inflammatory) or zinc salts (for acne and oil control). Topical zinc has real evidence for inflammatory and acne-prone skin, and zinc deficiency is associated with barrier problems. Very well-tolerated across skin types.

name: Zinc ­Gluconate
explanation: A zinc salt used in acne products for its antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory properties. Gentler than benzoyl peroxide and without the bleaching side effect. Some evidence supports it as an effective alternative to topical antibiotic acne treatments. Well-tolerated for reactive skin.

name: Zinc Oxide
explanation: The benchmark mineral sunscreen filter — it physically deflects UV radiation rather than absorbing it chemically. The most reactive-skin-friendly sunscreen option available: no systemic absorption, no hormone disruption concerns, no itching or stinging. The main drawback is white cast, though micronized formulas have reduced this substantially.

name: Zinc Stearate
explanation: The zinc salt of stearic acid, used as a thickener, lubricant, and slip agent in powder cosmetics. It helps products spread smoothly and prevents caking. Very small amounts are used in formulations, and zinc stearate has low sensitization potential — a background ingredient that's unlikely to be the source of any reaction.

name: Zingiber Officinale Extract
explanation: Ginger root extract — antioxidant and anti-inflammatory. Well-tolerated at extract concentrations.
